verses.kjv: FORASMUCH as many have taken in hand to set forth in order a declaration of those things which are most surely believed among us,
verses.bsb: Many have undertaken to compose an account of the things that have been fulfilled among us,

verses.kjv: Even as they delivered them unto us, which from the beginning were eyewitnesses, and ministers of the word;
verses.bsb: just as they were handed down to us by the initial eyewitnesses and servants of the word.

verses.kjv: It seemed good to me also, having had perfect understanding of all things from the very first, to write unto thee in order, most excellent Theophilus,
verses.bsb: Therefore, having carefully investigated everything from the beginning, it seemed good also to me to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,

verses.kjv: That thou mightest know the certainty of those things, wherein thou hast been instructed.
verses.bsb: so that you may know the certainty of the things you have been taught.

verses.kjv: ¶ THERE was in the days of Herod, the king of Judæa, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the course of Abia: and his wife was of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elisabeth.
verses.bsb: In the time of Herod king of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah, and whose wife Elizabeth was a daughter of Aaron.

verses.kjv: And they were both righteous before God, walking in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blameless.
verses.bsb: Both of them were righteous in the sight of God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and decrees of the Lord.

verses.kjv: And they had no child, because that Elisabeth was barren, and they both were now well stricken in years.
verses.bsb: But they had no children, because Elizabeth was barren, and they were both well along in years.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that while he executed the priest’s office before God in the order of his course,
verses.bsb: One day while Zechariah’s division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God,

verses.kjv: According to the custom of the priest’s office, his lot was to burn incense when he went into the temple of the Lord.
verses.bsb: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter the temple of the Lord and burn incense.

verses.kjv: And the whole multitude of the people were praying without at the time of incense.
verses.bsb: And at the hour of the incense offering, the whole congregation was praying outside.

verses.kjv: And there appeared unto him an angel of the Lord standing on the right side of the altar of incense.
verses.bsb: Just then an angel of the Lord appeared to Zechariah, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.

verses.kjv: And when Zacharias saw him, he was troubled, and fear fell upon him.
verses.bsb: When Zechariah saw him, he was startled and gripped with fear.

verses.kjv: But the angel said unto him, Fear not, Zacharias: for thy prayer is heard; and thy wife Elisabeth shall bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name John.
verses.bsb: But the angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zechariah, because your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you are to give him the name John.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt have joy and gladness; and many shall rejoice at his birth.
verses.bsb: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many will rejoice at his birth,

verses.kjv: For he shall be great in the sight of the Lord, and shall drink neither wine nor strong drink; and he shall be filled with the Holy Ghost, even from his mother’s womb.
verses.bsb: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He shall never take wine or strong drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit even from his mother’s womb.

verses.kjv: And many of the children of Israel shall he turn to the Lord their God.
verses.bsb: Many of the sons of Israel he will turn back to the Lord their God.

verses.kjv: And he shall go before him in the spirit and power of Elias, to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
verses.bsb: And he will go on before the Lord in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the fathers to their children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous—to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”

verses.kjv: And Zacharias said unto the angel, Whereby shall I know this? for I am an old man, and my wife well stricken in years.
verses.bsb: “How can I be sure of this?” Zechariah asked the angel. “I am an old man, and my wife is well along in years.”

verses.kjv: And the angel answering said unto him, I am Gabriel, that stand in the presence of God; and am sent to speak unto thee, and to shew thee these glad tidings.
verses.bsb: “I am Gabriel,” replied the angel. “I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.

verses.kjv: And, behold, thou shalt be dumb, and not able to speak, until the day that these things shall be performed, because thou believest not my words, which shall be fulfilled in their season.
verses.bsb: And now you will be silent and unable to speak until the day this comes to pass, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled at their proper time.”

verses.kjv: And the people waited for Zacharias, and marvelled that he tarried so long in the temple.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah and wondering why he took so long in the temple.

verses.kjv: And when he came out, he could not speak unto them: and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple: for he beckoned unto them, and remained speechless.
verses.bsb: When he came out and was unable to speak to them, they realized he had seen a vision in the temple. He kept making signs to them but remained speechless.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, as soon as the days of his ministration were accomplished, he departed to his own house.
verses.bsb: And when the days of his service were complete, he returned home.

verses.kjv: And after those days his wife Elisabeth conceived, and hid herself five months, saying,
verses.bsb: After these days, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant and for five months remained in seclusion. She declared,

verses.kjv: Thus hath the Lord dealt with me in the days wherein he looked on me, to take away my reproach among men.
verses.bsb: “The Lord has done this for me. In these days He has shown me favor and taken away my disgrace among the people.”

verses.kjv: And in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God unto a city of Galilee, named Nazareth,
verses.bsb: In the sixth month, God sent the angel Gabriel to a town in Galilee called Nazareth,

verses.kjv: To a virgin espoused to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and the virgin’s name was Mary.
verses.bsb: to a virgin pledged in marriage to a man named Joseph, who was of the house of David. And the virgin’s name was Mary.

verses.kjv: And the angel came in unto her, and said, Hail, thou that art highly favoured, the Lord is with thee: blessed art thou among women.
verses.bsb: The angel appeared to her and said, “Greetings, you who are highly favored! The Lord is with you.”

verses.kjv: And when she saw him, she was troubled at his saying, and cast in her mind what manner of salutation this should be.
verses.bsb: Mary was greatly troubled at his words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.

verses.kjv: And the angel said unto her, Fear not, Mary: for thou hast found favour with God.
verses.bsb: So the angel told her, “Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.

verses.kjv: And, behold, thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and bring forth a son, and shalt call his name JESUS.
verses.bsb: Behold, you will conceive and give birth to a son, and you are to give Him the name Jesus.

verses.kjv: He shall be great, and shall be called the Son of the Highest: and the Lord God shall give unto him the throne of his father David:
verses.bsb: He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give Him the throne of His father David,

verses.kjv: And he shall reign over the house of Jacob for ever; and of his kingdom there shall be no end.
verses.bsb: and He will reign over the house of Jacob forever. His kingdom will never end!”

verses.kjv: Then said Mary unto the angel, How shall this be, seeing I know not a man?
verses.bsb: “How can this be,” Mary asked the angel, “since I am a virgin?”

verses.kjv: And the angel answered and said unto her, The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee: therefore also that holy thing which shall be born of thee shall be called the Son of God.
verses.bsb: The angel replied, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. So the Holy One to be born will be called the Son of God.

verses.kjv: And, behold, thy cousin Elisabeth, she hath also conceived a son in her old age: and this is the sixth month with her, who was called barren.
verses.bsb: Look, even Elizabeth your relative has conceived a son in her old age, and she who was called barren is in her sixth month.

verses.kjv: For with God nothing shall be impossible.
verses.bsb: For no word from God will ever fail.”

verses.kjv: And Mary said, Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it unto me according to thy word. And the angel departed from her.
verses.bsb: “I am the Lord’s servant,” Mary answered. “May it happen to me according to your word.” Then the angel left her.

verses.kjv: And Mary arose in those days, and went into the hill country with haste, into a city of Juda;
verses.bsb: In those days Mary got ready and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judah,

verses.kjv: And entered into the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elisabeth.
verses.bsb: where she entered the home of Zechariah and greeted Elizabeth.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, when Elisabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leaped in her womb; and Elisabeth was filled with the Holy Ghost:
verses.bsb: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.

verses.kjv: And she spake out with a loud voice, and said, Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.
verses.bsb: In a loud voice she exclaimed, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!

verses.kjv: And whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
verses.bsb: And why am I so honored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?

verses.kjv: For, lo, as soon as the voice of thy salutation sounded in mine ears, the babe leaped in my womb for joy.
verses.bsb: For as soon as the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy.

verses.kjv: And blessed is she that believed: for there shall be a performance of those things which were told her from the Lord.
verses.bsb: Blessed is she who has believed that the Lord’s word to her will be fulfilled.”

verses.kjv: And Mary said, My soul doth magnify the Lord,
verses.bsb: Then Mary said:  “My soul magnifies the Lord,

verses.kjv: And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.
verses.bsb: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior!

verses.kjv: For he hath regarded the low estate of his handmaiden: for, behold, from henceforth all generations shall call me blessed.
verses.bsb: For He has looked with favor on the humble state of His servant. From now on all generations will call me blessed.

verses.kjv: For he that is mighty hath done to me great things; and holy is his name.
verses.bsb: For the Mighty One has done great things for me. Holy is His name.

verses.kjv: And his mercy is on them that fear him from generation to generation.
verses.bsb: His mercy extends to those who fear Him, from generation to generation.

verses.kjv: He hath shewed strength with his arm; he hath scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.
verses.bsb: He has performed mighty deeds with His arm; He has scattered those who are proud in the thoughts of their hearts.

verses.kjv: He hath put down the mighty from their seats, and exalted them of low degree.
verses.bsb: He has brought down rulers from their thrones, but has exalted the humble.

verses.kjv: He hath filled the hungry with good things; and the rich he hath sent empty away.
verses.bsb: He has filled the hungry with good things, but has sent the rich away empty.

verses.kjv: He hath holpen his servant Israel, in remembrance of his mercy;
verses.bsb: He has helped His servant Israel, remembering to be merciful,

verses.kjv: As he spake to our fathers, to Abraham, and to his seed for ever.
verses.bsb: as He promised to our fathers, to Abraham and his descendants forever.”

verses.kjv: And Mary abode with her about three months, and returned to her own house.
verses.bsb: Mary stayed with Elizabeth for about three months and then returned home.

verses.kjv: Now Elisabeth’s full time came that she should be delivered; and she brought forth a son.
verses.bsb: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a son.

verses.kjv: And her neighbours and her cousins heard how the Lord had shewed great mercy upon her; and they rejoiced with her.
verses.bsb: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy, and they rejoiced with her.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child; and they called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.
verses.bsb: On the eighth day, when they came to circumcise the child, they were going to name him after his father Zechariah.

verses.kjv: And his mother answered and said, Not so; but he shall be called John.
verses.bsb: But his mother replied, “No! He shall be called John.”

verses.kjv: And they said unto her, There is none of thy kindred that is called by this name.
verses.bsb: They said to her, “There is no one among your relatives who bears this name.”

verses.kjv: And they made signs to his father, how he would have him called.
verses.bsb: So they made signs to his father to find out what he wanted to name the child.

verses.kjv: And he asked for a writing table, and wrote, saying, His name is John. And they marvelled all.
verses.bsb: Zechariah asked for a tablet and wrote, “His name is John.” And they were all amazed.

verses.kjv: And his mouth was opened immediately, and his tongue loosed, and he spake, and praised God.
verses.bsb: Immediately Zechariah’s mouth was opened and his tongue was released, and he began to speak, praising God.

verses.kjv: And fear came on all that dwelt round about them: and all these sayings were noised abroad throughout all the hill country of Judæa.
verses.bsb: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and people throughout the hill country of Judea were talking about these events.

verses.kjv: And all they that heard them laid them up in their hearts, saying, What manner of child shall this be! And the hand of the Lord was with him.
verses.bsb: And all who heard this wondered in their hearts and asked, “What then will this child become?” For the Lord’s hand was with him.

verses.kjv: And his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Ghost, and prophesied, saying,
verses.bsb: Then his father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied:

verses.kjv: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel; for he hath visited and redeemed his people,
verses.bsb: “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, because He has visited and redeemed His people.

verses.kjv: And hath raised up an horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David;
verses.bsb: He has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of His servant David,

verses.kjv: As he spake by the mouth of his holy prophets, which have been since the world began:
verses.bsb: as He spoke through His holy prophets, those of ages past,

verses.kjv: That we should be saved from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate us;
verses.bsb: salvation from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us,

verses.kjv: To perform the mercy promised to our fathers, and to remember his holy covenant;
verses.bsb: to show mercy to our fathers and to remember His holy covenant,

verses.kjv: The oath which he sware to our father Abraham,
verses.bsb: the oath He swore to our father Abraham, to grant us

verses.kjv: That he would grant unto us, that we being delivered out of the hand of our enemies might serve him without fear,
verses.bsb: deliverance from hostile hands, that we may serve Him without fear,

verses.kjv: In holiness and righteousness before him, all the days of our life.
verses.bsb: in holiness and righteousness before Him all the days of our lives.

verses.kjv: And thou, child, shalt be called the prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways;
verses.bsb: And you, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High; for you will go on before the Lord to prepare the way for Him,

verses.kjv: To give knowledge of salvation unto his people by the remission of their sins,
verses.bsb: to give to His people the knowledge of salvation through the forgiveness of their sins,

verses.kjv: Through the tender mercy of our God; whereby the dayspring from on high hath visited us,
verses.bsb: because of the tender mercy of our God, by which the Dawn will visit us from on high,

verses.kjv: To give light to them that sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.
verses.bsb: to shine on those who live in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the path of peace.”

verses.kjv: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, and was in the deserts till the day of his shewing unto Israel.
verses.bsb: And the child grew and became strong in spirit; and he lived in the wilderness until the time of his public appearance to Israel.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass in those days, that there went out a decree from Cæsar Augustus, that all the world should be taxed.
verses.bsb: Now in those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that a census should be taken of the whole empire.

verses.kjv: (And this taxing was first made when Cyrenius was governor of Syria.)
verses.bsb: This was the first census to take place while Quirinius was governor of Syria.

verses.kjv: And all went to be taxed, every one into his own city.
verses.bsb: And everyone went to his own town to register.

verses.kjv: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judæa, unto the city of David, which is called Bethlehem; (because he was of the house and lineage of David:)
verses.bsb: So Joseph also went up from Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to the city of David called Bethlehem, since he was from the house and line of David.

verses.kjv: To be taxed with Mary his espoused wife, being great with child.
verses.bsb: He went there to register with Mary, who was pledged to him in marriage and was expecting a child.

verses.kjv: And so it was, that, while they were there, the days were accomplished that she should be delivered.
verses.bsb: While they were there, the time came for her Child to be born.

verses.kjv: And she brought forth her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger; because there was no room for them in the inn.
verses.bsb: And she gave birth to her firstborn, a Son. She wrapped Him in swaddling cloths and laid Him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.

verses.kjv: And there were in the same country shepherds abiding in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night.
verses.bsb: And there were shepherds residing in the fields nearby, keeping watch over their flocks by night.

verses.kjv: And, lo, the angel of the Lord came upon them, and the glory of the Lord shone round about them: and they were sore afraid.
verses.bsb: Just then an angel of the Lord stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.

verses.kjv: And the angel said unto them, Fear not: for, behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy, which shall be to all people.
verses.bsb: But the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid! For behold, I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people:

verses.kjv: For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Saviour, which is Christ the Lord.
verses.bsb: Today in the city of David a Savior has been born to you. He is Christ the Lord!

verses.kjv: And this shall be a sign unto you; Ye shall find the babe wrapped in swaddling clothes, lying in a manger.
verses.bsb: And this will be a sign to you: You will find a baby wrapped in swaddling cloths and lying in a manger.”

verses.kjv: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God, and saying,
verses.bsb: And suddenly there appeared with the angel a great multitude of the heavenly host, praising God and saying:

verses.kjv: Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.
verses.bsb: “Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace to men on whom His favor rests!”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as the angels were gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds said one to another, Let us now go even unto Bethlehem, and see this thing which is come to pass, which the Lord hath made known unto us.
verses.bsb: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let us go to Bethlehem and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”

verses.kjv: And they came with haste, and found Mary, and Joseph, and the babe lying in a manger.
verses.bsb: So they hurried off and found Mary and Joseph and the Baby, who was lying in the manger.

verses.kjv: And when they had seen it, they made known abroad the saying which was told them concerning this child.
verses.bsb: After they had seen the Child, they spread the message they had received about Him.

verses.kjv: And all they that heard it wondered at those things which were told them by the shepherds.
verses.bsb: And all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds said to them.

verses.kjv: But Mary kept all these things, and pondered them in her heart.
verses.bsb: But Mary treasured up all these things and pondered them in her heart.

verses.kjv: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, as it was told unto them.
verses.bsb: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen, which was just as the angel had told them.

verses.kjv: And when eight days were accomplished for the circumcising of the child, his name was called JESUS, which was so named of the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
verses.bsb: When the eight days until His circumcision had passed, He was named Jesus, the name the angel had given Him before He had been conceived.

verses.kjv: And when the days of her purification according to the law of Moses were accomplished, they brought him to Jerusalem, to present him to the Lord;
verses.bsb: And when the time of purification according to the Law of Moses was complete, His parents brought Him to Jerusalem to present Him to the Lord

verses.kjv: (As it is written in the law of the Lord, Every male that openeth the womb shall be called holy to the Lord;)
verses.bsb: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord: “Every firstborn male shall be consecrated to the Lord”),

verses.kjv: And to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.
verses.bsb: and to offer the sacrifice specified in the Law of the Lord: “A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons.”

verses.kjv: And, behold, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon; and the same man was just and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was upon him.
verses.bsb: Now there was a man in Jerusalem named Simeon, who was righteous and devout. He was waiting for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.

verses.kjv: And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
verses.bsb: The Holy Spirit had revealed to him that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.

verses.kjv: And he came by the Spirit into the temple: and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him after the custom of the law,
verses.bsb: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple courts. And when the parents brought in the child Jesus to do for Him what was customary under the Law,

verses.kjv: Then took he him up in his arms, and blessed God, and said,
verses.bsb: Simeon took Him in his arms and blessed God, saying:

verses.kjv: Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:
verses.bsb: “Sovereign Lord, as You have promised, You now dismiss Your servant in peace.

verses.kjv: For mine eyes have seen thy salvation,
verses.bsb: For my eyes have seen Your salvation,

verses.kjv: Which thou hast prepared before the face of all people;
verses.bsb: which You have prepared in the sight of all people,

verses.kjv: A light to lighten the Gentiles, and the glory of thy people Israel.
verses.bsb: a light for revelation to the Gentiles, and for glory to Your people Israel.”

verses.kjv: And Joseph and his mother marvelled at those things which were spoken of him.
verses.bsb: The Child’s father and mother were amazed at what was spoken about Him.

verses.kjv: And Simeon blessed them, and said unto Mary his mother, Behold, this child is set for the fall and rising again of many in Israel; and for a sign which shall be spoken against;
verses.bsb: Then Simeon blessed them and said to His mother Mary:  “Behold, this Child is appointed to cause the rise and fall of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be spoken against,

verses.kjv: (Yea, a sword shall pierce through thy own soul also,) that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.
verses.bsb: so that the thoughts of many hearts will be revealed— and a sword will pierce your soul as well.”

verses.kjv: And there was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Aser: she was of a great age, and had lived with an husband seven years from her virginity;
verses.bsb: There was also a prophetess named Anna, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher, who was well along in years. She had been married for seven years,

verses.kjv: And she was a widow of about fourscore and four years, which departed not from the temple, but served God with fastings and prayers night and day.
verses.bsb: and then was a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the temple, but worshiped night and day, fasting and praying.

verses.kjv: And she coming in that instant gave thanks likewise unto the Lord, and spake of him to all them that looked for redemption in Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Coming forward at that moment, she gave thanks to God and spoke about the Child to all who were waiting for the redemption of Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And when they had performed all things according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city Nazareth.
verses.bsb: When Jesus’ parents had done everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.

verses.kjv: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, filled with wisdom: and the grace of God was upon him.
verses.bsb: And the Child grew and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon Him.

verses.kjv: Now his parents went to Jerusalem every year at the feast of the passover.
verses.bsb: Every year His parents went to Jerusalem for the Feast of the Passover.

verses.kjv: And when he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem after the custom of the feast.
verses.bsb: And when He was twelve years old, they went up according to the custom of the Feast.

verses.kjv: And when they had fulfilled the days, as they returned, the child Jesus tarried behind in Jerusalem; and Joseph and his mother knew not of it.
verses.bsb: When those days were over and they were returning home, the boy Jesus remained behind in Jerusalem, but His parents were unaware He had stayed.

verses.kjv: But they, supposing him to have been in the company, went a day’s journey; and they sought him among their kinsfolk and acquaintance.
verses.bsb: Assuming He was in their company, they traveled on for a day before they began to look for Him among their relatives and friends.

verses.kjv: And when they found him not, they turned back again to Jerusalem, seeking him.
verses.bsb: When they could not find Him, they returned to Jerusalem to search for Him.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that after three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the doctors, both hearing them, and asking them questions.
verses.bsb: Finally, after three days they found Him in the temple courts, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.

verses.kjv: And all that heard him were astonished at his understanding and answers.
verses.bsb: And all who heard Him were astounded at His understanding and His answers.

verses.kjv: And when they saw him, they were amazed: and his mother said unto him, Son, why hast thou thus dealt with us? behold, thy father and I have sought thee sorrowing.
verses.bsb: When His parents saw Him, they were astonished. “Child, why have You done this to us?” His mother asked. “Your father and I have been anxiously searching for You.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, How is it that ye sought me? wist ye not that I must be about my Father’s business?
verses.bsb: “Why were you looking for Me?” He asked. “Did you not know that I had to be in My Father’s house?”

verses.kjv: And they understood not the saying which he spake unto them.
verses.bsb: But they did not understand the statement He was making to them.

verses.kjv: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth, and was subject unto them: but his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.
verses.bsb: Then He went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. But His mother treasured up all these things in her heart.

verses.kjv: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and man.
verses.bsb: And Jesus grew in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and man.

verses.kjv: NOW in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Cæsar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judæa, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of Ituræa and of the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanias the tetrarch of Abilene,
verses.bsb: In the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, while Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, Herod tetrarch of Galilee, his brother Philip tetrarch of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene,

verses.kjv: Annas and Caiaphas being the high priests, the word of God came unto John the son of Zacharias in the wilderness.
verses.bsb: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.

verses.kjv: And he came into all the country about Jordan, preaching the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins;
verses.bsb: He went into all the region around the Jordan, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins,

verses.kjv: As it is written in the book of the words of Esaias the prophet, saying, The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
verses.bsb: as it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet:  “A voice of one calling in the wilderness, ‘Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for Him.

verses.kjv: Every valley shall be filled, and every mountain and hill shall be brought low; and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough ways shall be made smooth;
verses.bsb: Every valley shall be filled in, and every mountain and hill made low. The crooked ways shall be made straight, and the rough ways smooth.

verses.kjv: And all flesh shall see the salvation of God.
verses.bsb: And all humanity will see God’s salvation.’”

verses.kjv: Then said he to the multitude that came forth to be baptized of him, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
verses.bsb: Then John said to the crowds coming out to be baptized by him, “You brood of vipers, who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?

verses.kjv: Bring forth therefore fruits worthy of repentance, and begin not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham to our father: for I say unto you, That God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.
verses.bsb: Produce fruit, then, in keeping with repentance. And do not begin to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father.’ For I tell you that out of these stones God can raise up children for Abraham.

verses.kjv: And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees: every tree therefore which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.
verses.bsb: The axe lies ready at the root of the trees, and every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.”

verses.kjv: And the people asked him, saying, What shall we do then?
verses.bsb: The crowds asked him, “What then should we do?”

verses.kjv: He answereth and saith unto them, He that hath two coats, let him impart to him that hath none; and he that hath meat, let him do likewise.
verses.bsb: John replied, “Whoever has two tunics should share with him who has none, and whoever has food should do the same.”

verses.kjv: Then came also publicans to be baptized, and said unto him, Master, what shall we do?
verses.bsb: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. “Teacher,” they asked, “what should we do?”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Exact no more than that which is appointed you.
verses.bsb: “Collect no more than you are authorized,” he answered.

verses.kjv: And the soldiers likewise demanded of him, saying, And what shall we do? And he said unto them, Do violence to no man, neither accuse any falsely; and be content with your wages.
verses.bsb: Then some soldiers asked him, “And what should we do?”  “Do not take money by force or false accusation,” he said. “Be content with your wages.”

verses.kjv: And as the people were in expectation, and all men mused in their hearts of John, whether he were the Christ, or not;
verses.bsb: The people were waiting expectantly and were all wondering in their hearts if John could be the Christ.

verses.kjv: John answered, saying unto them all, I indeed baptize you with water; but one mightier than I cometh, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to unloose: he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire:
verses.bsb: John answered all of them: “I baptize you with water, but One more powerful than I will come, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.

verses.kjv: Whose fan is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and will gather the wheat into his garner; but the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable.
verses.bsb: His winnowing fork is in His hand to clear His threshing floor and to gather the wheat into His barn; but He will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”

verses.kjv: And many other things in his exhortation preached he unto the people.
verses.bsb: With these and many other exhortations, John proclaimed the good news to the people.

verses.kjv: But Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him for Herodias his brother Philip’s wife, and for all the evils which Herod had done,
verses.bsb: But when he rebuked Herod the tetrarch regarding his brother’s wife Herodias and all the evils he had done,

verses.kjv: Added yet this above all, that he shut up John in prison.
verses.bsb: Herod added this to them all: He locked John up in prison.

verses.kjv: Now when all the people were baptized, it came to pass, that Jesus also being baptized, and praying, the heaven was opened,
verses.bsb: When all the people were being baptized, Jesus was baptized too. And as He was praying, heaven was opened,

verses.kjv: And the Holy Ghost descended in a bodily shape like a dove upon him, and a voice came from heaven, which said, Thou art my beloved Son; in thee I am well pleased.
verses.bsb: and the Holy Spirit descended on Him in a bodily form like a dove. And a voice came from heaven: “You are My beloved Son; in You I am well pleased.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus himself began to be about thirty years of age, being (as was supposed) the son of Joseph, which was the son of Heli,
verses.bsb: Jesus Himself was about thirty years old when He began His ministry.  He was regarded as the son of Joseph, the son of Heli,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Matthat, which was the son of Levi, which was the son of Melchi, which was the son of Janna, which was the son of Joseph,
verses.bsb: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Mattathias, which was the son of Amos, which was the son of Naum, which was the son of Esli, which was the son of Nagge,
verses.bsb: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Maath, which was the son of Mattathias, which was the son of Semei, which was the son of Joseph, which was the son of Juda,
verses.bsb: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Josech, the son of Joda,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Joanna, which was the son of Rhesa, which was the son of Zorobabel, which was the son of Salathiel, which was the son of Neri,
verses.bsb: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Melchi, which was the son of Addi, which was the son of Cosam, which was the son of Elmodam, which was the son of Er,
verses.bsb: the son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmadam, the son of Er,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Jose, which was the son of Eliezer, which was the son of Jorim, which was the son of Matthat, which was the son of Levi,
verses.bsb: the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Simeon, which was the son of Juda, which was the son of Joseph, which was the son of Jonan, which was the son of Eliakim,
verses.bsb: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Melea, which was the son of Menan, which was the son of Mattatha, which was the son of Nathan, which was the son of David,
verses.bsb: the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Jesse, which was the son of Obed, which was the son of Booz, which was the son of Salmon, which was the son of Naasson,
verses.bsb: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Sala, the son of Nahshon,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Aminadab, which was the son of Aram, which was the son of Esrom, which was the son of Phares, which was the son of Juda,
verses.bsb: the son of Amminadab, the son of Admin, the son of Arni, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Jacob, which was the son of Isaac, which was the son of Abraham, which was the son of Thara, which was the son of Nachor,
verses.bsb: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Saruch, which was the son of Ragau, which was the son of Phalec, which was the son of Heber, which was the son of Sala,
verses.bsb: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Cainan, which was the son of Arphaxad, which was the son of Sem, which was the son of Noe, which was the son of Lamech,
verses.bsb: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Mathusala, which was the son of Enoch, which was the son of Jared, which was the son of Maleleel, which was the son of Cainan,
verses.bsb: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalalel, the son of Cainan,

verses.kjv: Which was the son of Enos, which was the son of Seth, which was the son of Adam, which was the son of God.
verses.bsb: the son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.

verses.kjv: AND Jesus being full of the Holy Ghost returned from Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,
verses.bsb: Then Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,

verses.kjv: Being forty days tempted of the devil. And in those days he did eat nothing: and when they were ended, he afterward hungered.
verses.bsb: where for forty days He was tempted by the devil. He ate nothing during those days, and when they had ended, He was hungry.

verses.kjv: And the devil said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, command this stone that it be made bread.
verses.bsb: The devil said to Him, “If You are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered him, saying, It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.
verses.bsb: But Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone.’”

verses.kjv: And the devil, taking him up into an high mountain, shewed unto him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
verses.bsb: Then the devil led Him up to a high place and showed Him in an instant all the kingdoms of the world.

verses.kjv: And the devil said unto him, All this power will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is delivered unto me; and to whomsoever I will I give it.
verses.bsb: “I will give You authority over all these kingdoms and all their glory,” he said. “For it has been relinquished to me, and I can give it to anyone I wish.

verses.kjv: If thou therefore wilt worship me, all shall be thine.
verses.bsb: So if You worship me, it will all be Yours.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.
verses.bsb: But Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve Him only.’”

verses.kjv: And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on a pinnacle of the temple, and said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down from hence:
verses.bsb: Then the devil led Him to Jerusalem and set Him on the pinnacle of the temple. “If You are the Son of God,” he said, “throw Yourself down from here.

verses.kjv: For it is written, He shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee:
verses.bsb: For it is written:  ‘He will command His angels concerning You to guard You carefully;

verses.kjv: And in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.
verses.bsb: and they will lift You up in their hands, so that You will not strike Your foot against a stone.’”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.
verses.bsb: But Jesus answered, “It also says, ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’”

verses.kjv: And when the devil had ended all the temptation, he departed from him for a season.
verses.bsb: When the devil had finished every temptation, he left Him until an opportune time.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee: and there went out a fame of him through all the region round about.
verses.bsb: Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and the news about Him spread throughout the surrounding region.

verses.kjv: And he taught in their synagogues, being glorified of all.
verses.bsb: He taught in their synagogues and was glorified by everyone.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up: and, as his custom was, he went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and stood up for to read.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus came to Nazareth, where He had been brought up. As was His custom, He entered the synagogue on the Sabbath. And when He stood up to read,

verses.kjv: And there was delivered unto him the book of the prophet Esaias. And when he had opened the book, he found the place where it was written,
verses.bsb: the scroll of the prophet Isaiah was handed to Him. Unrolling it, He found the place where it was written:

verses.kjv: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised,
verses.bsb: “The Spirit of the Lord is on Me, because He has anointed Me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent Me to proclaim liberty to the captives and recovery of sight to the blind, to release the oppressed,

verses.kjv: To preach the acceptable year of the Lord.
verses.bsb: to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor.”

verses.kjv: And he closed the book, and he gave it again to the minister, and sat down. And the eyes of all them that were in the synagogue were fastened on him.
verses.bsb: Then He rolled up the scroll, returned it to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of everyone in the synagogue were fixed on Him,

verses.kjv: And he began to say unto them, This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.
verses.bsb: and He began by saying, “Today this Scripture is fulfilled in your hearing.”

verses.kjv: And all bare him witness, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth. And they said, Is not this Joseph’s son?
verses.bsb: All spoke well of Him and marveled at the gracious words that came from His lips. “Isn’t this the son of Joseph?” they asked.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to them, “Surely you will quote this proverb to Me: ‘Physician, heal yourself! Do here in Your hometown what we have heard that You did in Capernaum.’”

verses.kjv: And he said, Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country.
verses.bsb: Then He added, “Truly I tell you, no prophet is accepted in his hometown.

verses.kjv: But I tell you of a truth, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when great famine was throughout all the land;
verses.bsb: But I tell you truthfully that there were many widows in Israel in the time of Elijah, when the sky was shut for three and a half years and great famine swept over all the land.

verses.kjv: But unto none of them was Elias sent, save unto Sarepta, a city of Sidon, unto a woman that was a widow.
verses.bsb: Yet Elijah was not sent to any of them, but to the widow of Zarephath in Sidon.

verses.kjv: And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Eliseus the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, saving Naaman the Syrian.
verses.bsb: And there were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet. Yet not one of them was cleansed—only Naaman the Syrian.”

verses.kjv: And all they in the synagogue, when they heard these things, were filled with wrath,
verses.bsb: On hearing this, all the people in the synagogue were enraged.

verses.kjv: And rose up, and thrust him out of the city, and led him unto the brow of the hill whereon their city was built, that they might cast him down headlong.
verses.bsb: They got up, drove Him out of the town, and led Him to the brow of the hill on which the town was built, in order to throw Him over the cliff.

verses.kjv: But he passing through the midst of them went his way,
verses.bsb: But Jesus passed through the crowd and went on His way.

verses.kjv: And came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and taught them on the sabbath days.
verses.bsb: Then He went down to Capernaum, a town in Galilee, and on the Sabbath He began to teach the people.

verses.kjv: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for his word was with power.
verses.bsb: They were astonished at His teaching, because His message had authority.

verses.kjv: ¶ And in the synagogue there was a man, which had a spirit of an unclean devil, and cried out with a loud voice,
verses.bsb: In the synagogue there was a man possessed by the spirit of an unclean demon. He cried out in a loud voice,

verses.kjv: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art; the Holy One of God.
verses.bsb: “Ha! What do You want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God!”

verses.kjv: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him. And when the devil had thrown him in the midst, he came out of him, and hurt him not.
verses.bsb: But Jesus rebuked the demon. “Be silent!” He said. “Come out of him!” At this, the demon threw the man down before them all and came out without harming him.

verses.kjv: And they were all amazed, and spake among themselves, saying, What a word is this! for with authority and power he commandeth the unclean spirits, and they come out.
verses.bsb: All the people were overcome with amazement and asked one another, “What is this message? With authority and power He commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!”

verses.kjv: And the fame of him went out into every place of the country round about.
verses.bsb: And the news about Jesus spread throughout the surrounding region.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he arose out of the synagogue, and entered into Simon’s house. And Simon’s wife’s mother was taken with a great fever; and they besought him for her.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had left the synagogue, He went to the home of Simon, whose mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever. So they appealed to Jesus on her behalf,

verses.kjv: And he stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her: and immediately she arose and ministered unto them.
verses.bsb: and He stood over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. And she got up at once and began to serve them.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now when the sun was setting, all they that had any sick with divers diseases brought them unto him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.
verses.bsb: At sunset, all who were ill with various diseases were brought to Jesus, and laying His hands on each one, He healed them.

verses.kjv: And devils also came out of many, crying out, and saying, Thou art Christ the Son of God. And he rebuking them suffered them not to speak: for they knew that he was Christ.
verses.bsb: Demons also came out of many people, shouting, “You are the Son of God!” But He rebuked the demons and would not allow them to speak, because they knew He was the Christ.

verses.kjv: And when it was day, he departed and went into a desert place: and the people sought him, and came unto him, and stayed him, that he should not depart from them.
verses.bsb: At daybreak, Jesus went out to a solitary place, and the crowds were looking for Him. They came to Him and tried to keep Him from leaving.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.
verses.bsb: But Jesus told them, “I must preach the good news of the kingdom of God to the other towns as well, because that is why I was sent.”

verses.kjv: And he preached in the synagogues of Galilee.
verses.bsb: And He continued to preach in the synagogues of Judea.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, that, as the people pressed upon him to hear the word of God, he stood by the lake of Gennesaret,
verses.bsb: On one occasion, while Jesus was standing by the Lake of Gennesaret with the crowd pressing in on Him to hear the word of God,

verses.kjv: And saw two ships standing by the lake: but the fishermen were gone out of them, and were washing their nets.
verses.bsb: He saw two boats at the edge of the lake. The fishermen had left them and were washing their nets.

verses.kjv: And he entered into one of the ships, which was Simon’s, and prayed him that he would thrust out a little from the land. And he sat down, and taught the people out of the ship.
verses.bsb: Jesus got into the boat belonging to Simon and asked him to put out a little from shore. And sitting down, He taught the people from the boat.

verses.kjv: Now when he had left speaking, he said unto Simon, Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.
verses.bsb: When Jesus had finished speaking, He said to Simon, “Put out into deep water and let down your nets for a catch.”

verses.kjv: And Simon answering said unto him, Master, we have toiled all the night, and have taken nothing: nevertheless at thy word I will let down the net.
verses.bsb: “Master,” Simon replied, “we have worked hard all night without catching anything. But because You say so, I will let down the nets.”

verses.kjv: And when they had this done, they inclosed a great multitude of fishes: and their net brake.
verses.bsb: When they had done so, they caught such a large number of fish that their nets began to tear.

verses.kjv: And they beckoned unto their partners, which were in the other ship, that they should come and help them. And they came, and filled both the ships, so that they began to sink.
verses.bsb: So they signaled to their partners in the other boat to come and help them, and they came and filled both boats so full that they began to sink.

verses.kjv: When Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, Depart from me; for I am a sinful man, O Lord.
verses.bsb: When Simon Peter saw this, he fell at Jesus’ knees. “Go away from me, Lord,” he said, “for I am a sinful man.”

verses.kjv: For he was astonished, and all that were with him, at the draught of the fishes which they had taken:
verses.bsb: For he and his companions were astonished at the catch of fish they had taken,

verses.kjv: And so was also James, and John, the sons of Zebedee, which were partners with Simon. And Jesus said unto Simon, Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.
verses.bsb: and so were his partners James and John, the sons of Zebedee.  “Do not be afraid,” Jesus said to Simon. “From now on you will catch men.”

verses.kjv: And when they had brought their ships to land, they forsook all, and followed him.
verses.bsb: And when they had brought their boats ashore, they left everything and followed Him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, when he was in a certain city, behold a man full of leprosy: who seeing Jesus fell on his face, and besought him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
verses.bsb: While Jesus was in one of the towns, a man came along who was covered with leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell facedown and begged Him, “Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.”

verses.kjv: And he put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will: be thou clean. And immediately the leprosy departed from him.
verses.bsb: Jesus reached out His hand and touched the man. “I am willing,” He said. “Be clean!” And immediately the leprosy left him.

verses.kjv: And he charged him to tell no man: but go, and shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
verses.bsb: “Do not tell anyone,” Jesus instructed him. “But go, show yourself to the priest and present the offering Moses prescribed for your cleansing, as a testimony to them.”

verses.kjv: But so much the more went there a fame abroad of him: and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.
verses.bsb: But the news about Jesus spread all the more, and great crowds came to hear Him and to be healed of their sicknesses.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he withdrew himself into the wilderness, and prayed.
verses.bsb: Yet He frequently withdrew to the wilderness to pray.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass on a certain day, as he was teaching, that there were Pharisees and doctors of the law sitting by, which were come out of every town of Galilee, and Judæa, and Jerusalem: and the power of the Lord was present to heal them.
verses.bsb: One day Jesus was teaching, and the Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting there. People had come from Jerusalem and from every village of Galilee and Judea, and the power of the Lord was present for Him to heal the sick.

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, men brought in a bed a man which was taken with a palsy: and they sought means to bring him in, and to lay him before him.
verses.bsb: Just then some men came carrying a paralyzed man on a mat. They tried to bring him inside to set him before Jesus,

verses.kjv: And when they could not find by what way they might bring him in because of the multitude, they went upon the housetop, and let him down through the tiling with his couch into the midst before Jesus.
verses.bsb: but they could not find a way through the crowd. So they went up on the roof and lowered him on his mat through the tiles into the middle of the crowd, right in front of Jesus.

verses.kjv: And when he saw their faith, he said unto him, Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw their faith, He said, “Friend, your sins are forgiven.”

verses.kjv: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, Who is this which speaketh blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?
verses.bsb: But the scribes and Pharisees began thinking to themselves, “Who is this man who speaks blasphemy? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”

verses.kjv: But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answering said unto them, What reason ye in your hearts?
verses.bsb: Knowing what they were thinking, Jesus replied, “Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?

verses.kjv: Whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Rise up and walk?
verses.bsb: Which is easier: to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk?’

verses.kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power upon earth to forgive sins, (he said unto the sick of the palsy,) I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy couch, and go into thine house.
verses.bsb: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on the earth to forgive sins...” He said to the paralytic, “I tell you, get up, pick up your mat, and go home.”

verses.kjv: And immediately he rose up before them, and took up that whereon he lay, and departed to his own house, glorifying God.
verses.bsb: And immediately the man stood up before them, took what he had been lying on, and went home glorifying God.

verses.kjv: And they were all amazed, and they glorified God, and were filled with fear, saying, We have seen strange things to day.
verses.bsb: Everyone was taken with amazement and glorified God. They were filled with awe and said, “We have seen remarkable things today.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And after these things he went forth, and saw a publican, named Levi, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he said unto him, Follow me.
verses.bsb: After this, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax booth. “Follow Me,” He told him,

verses.kjv: And he left all, rose up, and followed him.
verses.bsb: and Levi got up, left everything, and followed Him.

verses.kjv: And Levi made him a great feast in his own house: and there was a great company of publicans and of others that sat down with them.
verses.bsb: Then Levi hosted a great banquet for Jesus at his house. A large crowd of tax collectors was there, along with others who were eating with them.

verses.kjv: But their scribes and Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, Why do ye eat and drink with publicans and sinners?
verses.bsb: But the Pharisees and their scribes complained to Jesus’ disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, They that are whole need not a physician; but they that are sick.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.

verses.kjv: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
verses.bsb: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners, to repentance.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And they said unto him, Why do the disciples of John fast often, and make prayers, and likewise the disciples of the Pharisees; but thine eat and drink?
verses.bsb: Then they said to Him, “John’s disciples and those of the Pharisees frequently fast and pray, but Yours keep on eating and drinking.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Can ye make the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them?
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Can you make the guests of the bridegroom fast while He is with them?

verses.kjv: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
verses.bsb: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he spake also a parable unto them; No man putteth a piece of a new garment upon an old; if otherwise, then both the new maketh a rent, and the piece that was taken out of the new agreeth not with the old.
verses.bsb: He also told them a parable: “No one tears a piece of cloth from a new garment and sews it on an old one. If he does, he will tear the new garment as well, and the patch from the new will not match the old.

verses.kjv: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles; else the new wine will burst the bottles, and be spilled, and the bottles shall perish.
verses.bsb: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the new wine will burst the skins, the wine will spill, and the wineskins will be ruined.

verses.kjv: But new wine must be put into new bottles; and both are preserved.
verses.bsb: Instead, new wine is poured into new wineskins.

verses.kjv: No man also having drunk old wine straightway desireth new: for he saith, The old is better.
verses.bsb: And no one after drinking old wine wants new, for he says, ‘The old is better.’”

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass on the second sabbath after the first, that he went through the corn fields; and his disciples plucked the ears of corn, and did eat, rubbing them in their hands.
verses.bsb: One Sabbath Jesus was passing through the grainfields, and His disciples began to pick the heads of grain, rub them in their hands, and eat them.

verses.kjv: And certain of the Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the sabbath days?
verses.bsb: But some of the Pharisees asked, “Why are you doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering them said, Have ye not read so much as this, what David did, when himself was an hungred, and they which were with him;
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?

verses.kjv: How he went into the house of God, and did take and eat the shewbread, and gave also to them that were with him; which it is not lawful to eat but for the priests alone?
verses.bsb: He entered the house of God, took the consecrated bread and gave it to his companions, and ate what is lawful only for the priests to eat.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, That the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus declared, “The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass also on another sabbath, that he entered into the synagogue and taught: and there was a man whose right hand was withered.
verses.bsb: On another Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and was teaching, and a man was there whose right hand was withered.

verses.kjv: And the scribes and Pharisees watched him, whether he would heal on the sabbath day; that they might find an accusation against him.
verses.bsb: Looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, the scribes and Pharisees were watching Him closely to see if He would heal on the Sabbath.

verses.kjv: But he knew their thoughts, and said to the man which had the withered hand, Rise up, and stand forth in the midst. And he arose and stood forth.
verses.bsb: But Jesus knew their thoughts and said to the man with the withered hand, “Get up and stand among us.” So he got up and stood there.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, I will ask you one thing; Is it lawful on the sabbath days to do good, or to do evil? to save life, or to destroy it?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to them, “I ask you, which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it?”

verses.kjv: And looking round about upon them all, he said unto the man, Stretch forth thy hand. And he did so: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
verses.bsb: And after looking around at all of them, He said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did so, and it was restored.

verses.kjv: And they were filled with madness; and communed one with another what they might do to Jesus.
verses.bsb: But the scribes and Pharisees were filled with rage and began to discuss with one another what they might do to Jesus.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that he went out into a mountain to pray, and continued all night in prayer to God.
verses.bsb: In those days Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and He spent the night in prayer to God.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when it was day, he called unto him his disciples: and of them he chose twelve, whom also he named apostles;
verses.bsb: When daylight came, He called His disciples to Him and chose twelve of them, whom He also designated as apostles:

verses.kjv: Simon, (whom he also named Peter,) and Andrew his brother, James and John, Philip and Bartholomew,
verses.bsb: Simon, whom He named Peter, and his brother Andrew; James and John; Philip and Bartholomew;

verses.kjv: Matthew and Thomas, James the son of Alphæus, and Simon called Zelotes,
verses.bsb: Matthew and Thomas; James son of Alphaeus and Simon called the Zealot;

verses.kjv: And Judas the brother of James, and Judas Iscariot, which also was the traitor.
verses.bsb: Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he came down with them, and stood in the plain, and the company of his disciples, and a great multitude of people out of all Judæa and Jerusalem, and from the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, which came to hear him, and to be healed of their diseases;
verses.bsb: Then Jesus came down with them and stood on a level place. A large crowd of His disciples was there, along with a great number of people from all over Judea, Jerusalem, and the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon.

verses.kjv: And they that were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed.
verses.bsb: They had come to hear Him and to be healed of their diseases, and those troubled by unclean spirits were healed.

verses.kjv: And the whole multitude sought to touch him: for there went virtue out of him, and healed them all.
verses.bsb: The entire crowd was trying to touch Him, because power was coming from Him and healing them all.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he lifted up his eyes on his disciples, and said, Blessed be ye poor: for yours is the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: Looking up at His disciples, Jesus said:  “Blessed are you who are poor, for yours is the kingdom of God.

verses.kjv: Blessed are ye that hunger now: for ye shall be filled. Blessed are ye that weep now: for ye shall laugh.
verses.bsb: Blessed are you who hunger now, for you will be filled. Blessed are you who weep now, for you will laugh.

verses.kjv: Blessed are ye, when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you from their company, and shall reproach you, and cast out your name as evil, for the Son of man’s sake.
verses.bsb: Blessed are you when people hate you, and when they exclude you and insult you and reject your name as evil because of the Son of Man.

verses.kjv: Rejoice ye in that day, and leap for joy: for, behold, your reward is great in heaven: for in the like manner did their fathers unto the prophets.
verses.bsb: Rejoice in that day and leap for joy, because great is your reward in heaven. For their fathers treated the prophets in the same way.

verses.kjv: But woe unto you that are rich! for ye have received your consolation.
verses.bsb: But woe to you who are rich, for you have already received your comfort.

verses.kjv: Woe unto you that are full! for ye shall hunger. Woe unto you that laugh now! for ye shall mourn and weep.
verses.bsb: Woe to you who are well fed now, for you will hunger. Woe to you who laugh now, for you will mourn and weep.

verses.kjv: Woe unto you, when all men shall speak well of you! for so did their fathers to the false prophets.
verses.bsb: Woe to you when all men speak well of you, for their fathers treated the false prophets in the same way.

verses.kjv: ¶ But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you,
verses.bsb: But to those of you who will listen, I say: Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,

verses.kjv: Bless them that curse you, and pray for them which despitefully use you.
verses.bsb: bless those who curse you, pray for those who mistreat you.

verses.kjv: And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek offer also the other; and him that taketh away thy cloke forbid not to take thy coat also.
verses.bsb: If someone strikes you on one cheek, turn to him the other also. And if someone takes your cloak, do not withhold your tunic as well.

verses.kjv: Give to every man that asketh of thee; and of him that taketh away thy goods ask them not again.
verses.bsb: Give to everyone who asks you, and if anyone takes what is yours, do not demand it back.

verses.kjv: And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.
verses.bsb: Do to others as you would have them do to you.

verses.kjv: For if ye love them which love you, what thank have ye? for sinners also love those that love them.
verses.bsb: If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners love those who love them.

verses.kjv: And if ye do good to them which do good to you, what thank have ye? for sinners also do even the same.
verses.bsb: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners do the same.

verses.kjv: And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive, what thank have ye? for sinners also lend to sinners, to receive as much again.
verses.bsb: And if you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, expecting to be repaid in full.

verses.kjv: But love ye your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again; and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be the children of the Highest: for he is kind unto the unthankful and to the evil.
verses.bsb: But love your enemies, do good to them, and lend to them, expecting nothing in return. Then your reward will be great, and you will be sons of the Most High; for He is kind to the ungrateful and wicked.

verses.kjv: Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father also is merciful.
verses.bsb: Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.

verses.kjv: Judge not, and ye shall not be judged: condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgive, and ye shall be forgiven:
verses.bsb: Do not judge, and you will not be judged. Do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.

verses.kjv: Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again.
verses.bsb: Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured back to you.”

verses.kjv: And he spake a parable unto them, Can the blind lead the blind? shall they not both fall into the ditch?
verses.bsb: Jesus also told them a parable: “Can a blind man lead a blind man? Will they not both fall into a pit?

verses.kjv: The disciple is not above his master: but every one that is perfect shall be as his master.
verses.bsb: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like his teacher.

verses.kjv: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother’s eye, but perceivest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
verses.bsb: Why do you look at the speck in your brother’s eye, but fail to notice the beam in your own eye?

verses.kjv: Either how canst thou say to thy brother, Brother, let me pull out the mote that is in thine eye, when thou thyself beholdest not the beam that is in thine own eye? Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine own eye, and then shalt thou see clearly to pull out the mote that is in thy brother’s eye.
verses.bsb: How can you say, ‘Brother, let me take the speck out of your eye,’ while you yourself fail to see the beam in your own eye? You hypocrite! First take the beam out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.

verses.kjv: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit; neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
verses.bsb: No good tree bears bad fruit, nor does a bad tree bear good fruit.

verses.kjv: For every tree is known by his own fruit. For of thorns men do not gather figs, nor of a bramble bush gather they grapes.
verses.bsb: For each tree is known by its own fruit. Indeed, figs are not gathered from thornbushes, nor grapes from brambles.

verses.kjv: A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is good; and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is evil: for of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh.
verses.bsb: The good man brings good things out of the good treasure of his heart, and the evil man brings evil things out of the evil treasure of his heart. For out of the overflow of the heart, the mouth speaks.

verses.kjv: ¶ And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things which I say?
verses.bsb: Why do you call Me ‘Lord, Lord,’ but not do what I say?

verses.kjv: Whosoever cometh to me, and heareth my sayings, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like:
verses.bsb: I will show you what he is like who comes to Me and hears My words and acts on them:

verses.kjv: He is like a man which built an house, and digged deep, and laid the foundation on a rock: and when the flood arose, the stream beat vehemently upon that house, and could not shake it: for it was founded upon a rock.
verses.bsb: He is like a man building a house, who dug down deep and laid his foundation on the rock. When the flood came, the torrent crashed against that house but could not shake it, because it was well built.

verses.kjv: But he that heareth, and doeth not, is like a man that without a foundation built an house upon the earth; against which the stream did beat vehemently, and immediately it fell; and the ruin of that house was great.
verses.bsb: But the one who hears My words and does not act on them is like a man who built his house on ground without a foundation. The torrent crashed against that house, and immediately it fell—and great was its destruction!”

verses.kjv: NOW when he had ended all his sayings in the audience of the people, he entered into Capernaum.
verses.bsb: When Jesus had concluded His discourse in the hearing of the people, He went to Capernaum.

verses.kjv: And a certain centurion’s servant, who was dear unto him, was sick, and ready to die.
verses.bsb: There a highly valued servant of a centurion was sick and about to die.

verses.kjv: And when he heard of Jesus, he sent unto him the elders of the Jews, beseeching him that he would come and heal his servant.
verses.bsb: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to ask Him to come and heal his servant.

verses.kjv: And when they came to Jesus, they besought him instantly, saying, That he was worthy for whom he should do this:
verses.bsb: They came to Jesus and pleaded with Him earnestly, “This man is worthy to have You grant this,

verses.kjv: For he loveth our nation, and he hath built us a synagogue.
verses.bsb: for he loves our nation and has built our synagogue.”

verses.kjv: Then Jesus went with them. And when he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying unto him, Lord, trouble not thyself: for I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof:
verses.bsb: So Jesus went with them. But when He was not far from the house, the centurion sent friends with the message: “Lord, do not trouble Yourself, for I am not worthy to have You come under my roof.

verses.kjv: Wherefore neither thought I myself worthy to come unto thee: but say in a word, and my servant shall be healed.
verses.bsb: That is why I did not consider myself worthy to come to You. But just say the word, and my servant will be healed.

verses.kjv: For I also am a man set under authority, having under me soldiers, and I say unto one, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.
verses.bsb: For I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell one to go, and he goes; and another to come, and he comes. I tell my servant to do something, and he does it.”

verses.kjv: When Jesus heard these things, he marvelled at him, and turned him about, and said unto the people that followed him, I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.
verses.bsb: When Jesus heard this, He marveled at the centurion. Turning to the crowd following Him, He said, “I tell you, not even in Israel have I found such great faith.”

verses.kjv: And they that were sent, returning to the house, found the servant whole that had been sick.
verses.bsb: And when the messengers returned to the house, they found the servant in good health.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass the day after, that he went into a city called Nain; and many of his disciples went with him, and much people.
verses.bsb: Soon afterward, Jesus went to a town called Nain. His disciples went with Him, accompanied by a large crowd.

verses.kjv: Now when he came nigh to the gate of the city, behold, there was a dead man carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow: and much people of the city was with her.
verses.bsb: As He approached the town gate, He saw a dead man being carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. And a large crowd from the town was with her.

verses.kjv: And when the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said unto her, Weep not.
verses.bsb: When the Lord saw her, He had compassion on her and said, “Do not weep.”

verses.kjv: And he came and touched the bier: and they that bare him stood still. And he said, Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.
verses.bsb: Then He went up and touched the coffin, and those carrying it stood still. “Young man,” He said, “I tell you, get up!”

verses.kjv: And he that was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he delivered him to his mother.
verses.bsb: And the dead man sat up and began to speak! Then Jesus gave him back to his mother.

verses.kjv: And there came a fear on all: and they glorified God, saying, That a great prophet is risen up among us; and, That God hath visited his people.
