verses.bsb: A sense of awe swept over all of them, and they glorified God. “A great prophet has appeared among us!” they said. “God has visited His people!”

verses.kjv: And this rumour of him went forth throughout all Judæa, and throughout all the region round about.
verses.bsb: And the news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and all the surrounding region.

verses.kjv: And the disciples of John shewed him of all these things.
verses.bsb: Then John’s disciples informed him about all these things.

verses.kjv: ¶ And John calling unto him two of his disciples sent them to Jesus, saying, Art thou he that should come? or look we for another?
verses.bsb: So John called two of his disciples and sent them to ask the Lord, “Are You the One who was to come, or should we look for someone else?”

verses.kjv: When the men were come unto him, they said, John Baptist hath sent us unto thee, saying, Art thou he that should come? or look we for another?
verses.bsb: When the men came to Jesus, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to ask, ‘Are You the One who was to come, or should we look for someone else?’”

verses.kjv: And in that same hour he cured many of their infirmities and plagues, and of evil spirits; and unto many that were blind he gave sight.
verses.bsb: At that very hour Jesus healed many people of their diseases, afflictions, and evil spirits, and He gave sight to many who were blind.

verses.kjv: Then Jesus answering said unto them, Go your way, and tell John what things ye have seen and heard; how that the blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, to the poor the gospel is preached.
verses.bsb: So He replied, “Go back and report to John what you have seen and heard: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is preached to the poor.

verses.kjv: And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the one who does not fall away on account of Me.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when the messengers of John were departed, he began to speak unto the people concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness for to see? A reed shaken with the wind?
verses.bsb: After John’s messengers had left, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swaying in the wind?

verses.kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? Behold, they which are gorgeously apparelled, and live delicately, are in kings’ courts.
verses.bsb: Otherwise, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? Look, those who wear elegant clothing and live in luxury are found in palaces.

verses.kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? Yea, I say unto you, and much more than a prophet.
verses.bsb: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.

verses.kjv: This is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
verses.bsb: This is the one about whom it is written:  ‘Behold, I will send My messenger ahead of You, who will prepare Your way before You.’

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, Among those that are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptist: but he that is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.
verses.bsb: I tell you, among those born of women there is no one greater than John, yet even the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.”

verses.kjv: And all the people that heard him, and the publicans, justified God, being baptized with the baptism of John.
verses.bsb: All the people who heard this, even the tax collectors, acknowledged God’s justice. For they had received the baptism of John.

verses.kjv: But the Pharisees and lawyers rejected the counsel of God against themselves, being not baptized of him.
verses.bsb: But the Pharisees and experts in the law rejected God’s purpose for themselves, because they had not been baptized by John.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said, Whereunto then shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like?
verses.bsb: “To what, then, can I compare the men of this generation? What are they like?

verses.kjv: They are like unto children sitting in the marketplace, and calling one to another, and saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned to you, and ye have not wept.
verses.bsb: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling out to one another:  ‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not weep.’

verses.kjv: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and ye say, He hath a devil.
verses.bsb: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon!’

verses.kjv: The Son of man is come eating and drinking; and ye say, Behold a gluttonous man, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners!
verses.bsb: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Look at this glutton and drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’

verses.kjv: But wisdom is justified of all her children.
verses.bsb: But wisdom is vindicated by all her children.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And one of the Pharisees desired him that he would eat with him. And he went into the Pharisee’s house, and sat down to meat.
verses.bsb: Then one of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him, and He entered the Pharisee’s house and reclined at the table.

verses.kjv: And, behold, a woman in the city, which was a sinner, when she knew that Jesus sat at meat in the Pharisee’s house, brought an alabaster box of ointment,
verses.bsb: When a sinful woman from that town learned that Jesus was dining there, she brought an alabaster jar of perfume.

verses.kjv: And stood at his feet behind him weeping, and began to wash his feet with tears, and did wipe them with the hairs of her head, and kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.
verses.bsb: As she stood behind Him at His feet weeping, she began to wet His feet with her tears and wipe them with her hair. Then she kissed His feet and anointed them with the perfume.

verses.kjv: Now when the Pharisee which had bidden him saw it, he spake within himself, saying, This man, if he were a prophet, would have known who and what manner of woman this is that toucheth him: for she is a sinner.
verses.bsb: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this, he said to himself, “If this man were a prophet, He would know who this is and what kind of woman is touching Him—for she is a sinner!”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, Simon, I have somewhat to say unto thee. And he saith, Master, say on.
verses.bsb: But Jesus answered him, “Simon, I have something to tell you.”  “Tell me, Teacher,” he said.

verses.kjv: There was a certain creditor which had two debtors: the one owed five hundred pence, and the other fifty.
verses.bsb: “Two men were debtors to a certain moneylender. One owed him five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.

verses.kjv: And when they had nothing to pay, he frankly forgave them both. Tell me therefore, which of them will love him most?
verses.bsb: When they were unable to repay him, he forgave both of them. Which one, then, will love him more?”

verses.kjv: Simon answered and said, I suppose that he, to whom he forgave most. And he said unto him, Thou hast rightly judged.
verses.bsb: “I suppose the one who was forgiven more,” Simon replied.  “You have judged correctly,” Jesus said.

verses.kjv: And he turned to the woman, and said unto Simon, Seest thou this woman? I entered into thine house, thou gavest me no water for my feet: but she hath washed my feet with tears, and wiped them with the hairs of her head.
verses.bsb: And turning toward the woman, He said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? When I entered your house, you did not give Me water for My feet, but she wet My feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair.

verses.kjv: Thou gavest me no kiss: but this woman since the time I came in hath not ceased to kiss my feet.
verses.bsb: You did not greet Me with a kiss, but she has not stopped kissing My feet since I arrived.

verses.kjv: My head with oil thou didst not anoint: but this woman hath anointed my feet with ointment.
verses.bsb: You did not anoint My head with oil, but she has anointed My feet with perfume.

verses.kjv: Wherefore I say unto thee, Her sins, which are many, are forgiven; for she loved much: but to whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little.
verses.bsb: Therefore I tell you, because her many sins have been forgiven, she has loved much. But he who has been forgiven little loves little.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto her, Thy sins are forgiven.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”

verses.kjv: And they that sat at meat with him began to say within themselves, Who is this that forgiveth sins also?
verses.bsb: But those at the table began to say to themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”

verses.kjv: And he said to the woman, Thy faith hath saved thee; go in peace.
verses.bsb: And Jesus told the woman, “Your faith has saved you; go in peace.”

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass afterward, that he went throughout every city and village, preaching and shewing the glad tidings of the kingdom of God: and the twelve were with him,
verses.bsb: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled from one town and village to another, preaching and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom of God. The Twelve were with Him,

verses.kjv: And certain women, which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities, Mary called Magdalene, out of whom went seven devils,
verses.bsb: as well as some women who had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,

verses.kjv: And Joanna the wife of Chuza Herod’s steward, and Susanna, and many others, which ministered unto him of their substance.
verses.bsb: Joanna the wife of Herod’s household manager Chuza, Susanna, and many others. These women were ministering to them out of their own means.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when much people were gathered together, and were come to him out of every city, he spake by a parable:
verses.bsb: While a large crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from town after town, He told them this parable:

verses.kjv: A sower went out to sow his seed: and as he sowed, some fell by the way side; and it was trodden down, and the fowls of the air devoured it.
verses.bsb: “A farmer went out to sow his seed. And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, where it was trampled, and the birds of the air devoured it.

verses.kjv: And some fell upon a rock; and as soon as it was sprung up, it withered away, because it lacked moisture.
verses.bsb: Some fell on rocky ground, and when it came up, the seedlings withered because they had no moisture.

verses.kjv: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprang up with it, and choked it.
verses.bsb: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up with it and choked the seedlings.

verses.kjv: And other fell on good ground, and sprang up, and bare fruit an hundredfold. And when he had said these things, he cried, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.bsb: Still other seed fell on good soil, where it sprang up and produced a crop—a hundredfold.”  As Jesus said this, He called out, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

verses.kjv: And his disciples asked him, saying, What might this parable be?
verses.bsb: Then His disciples asked Him what this parable meant.

verses.kjv: And he said, Unto you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God: but to others in parables; that seeing they might not see, and hearing they might not understand.
verses.bsb: He replied, “The knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of God has been given to you, but to others I speak in parables, so that,  ‘though seeing, they may not see; though hearing, they may not understand.’

verses.kjv: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
verses.bsb: Now this is the meaning of the parable: The seed is the word of God.

verses.kjv: Those by the way side are they that hear; then cometh the devil, and taketh away the word out of their hearts, lest they should believe and be saved.
verses.bsb: The seeds along the path are those who hear, but the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved.

verses.kjv: They on the rock are they, which, when they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no root, which for a while believe, and in time of temptation fall away.
verses.bsb: The seeds on rocky ground are those who hear the word and receive it with joy, but they have no root. They believe for a season, but in the time of testing, they fall away.

verses.kjv: And that which fell among thorns are they, which, when they have heard, go forth, and are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruit to perfection.
verses.bsb: The seeds that fell among the thorns are those who hear, but as they go on their way, they are choked by the worries, riches, and pleasures of this life, and their fruit does not mature.

verses.kjv: But that on the good ground are they, which in an honest and good heart, having heard the word, keep it, and bring forth fruit with patience.
verses.bsb: But the seeds on good soil are those with a noble and good heart, who hear the word, cling to it, and by persevering produce a crop.

verses.kjv: ¶ No man, when he hath lighted a candle, covereth it with a vessel, or putteth it under a bed; but setteth it on a candlestick, that they which enter in may see the light.
verses.bsb: No one lights a lamp and covers it with a jar or puts it under a bed. Instead, he sets it on a stand, so those who enter can see the light.

verses.kjv: For nothing is secret, that shall not be made manifest; neither any thing hid, that shall not be known and come abroad.
verses.bsb: For there is nothing hidden that will not be disclosed, and nothing concealed that will not be made known and brought to light.

verses.kjv: Take heed therefore how ye hear: for whosoever hath, to him shall be given; and whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he seemeth to have.
verses.bsb: Pay attention, therefore, to how you listen. Whoever has will be given more, but whoever does not have, even what he thinks he has will be taken away from him.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then came to him his mother and his brethren, and could not come at him for the press.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus’ mother and brothers came to see Him, but they were unable to reach Him because of the crowd.

verses.kjv: And it was told him by certain which said, Thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to see thee.
verses.bsb: He was told, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see You.”

verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto them, My mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of God, and do it.
verses.bsb: But He replied, “My mother and brothers are those who hear the word of God and carry it out.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Now it came to pass on a certain day, that he went into a ship with his disciples: and he said unto them, Let us go over unto the other side of the lake. And they launched forth.
verses.bsb: One day Jesus said to His disciples, “Let us cross to the other side of the lake.” So He got into a boat with them and set out.

verses.kjv: But as they sailed he fell asleep: and there came down a storm of wind on the lake; and they were filled with water, and were in jeopardy.
verses.bsb: As they sailed, He fell asleep, and a windstorm came down on the lake, so that the boat was being swamped, and they were in great danger.

verses.kjv: And they came to him, and awoke him, saying, Master, master, we perish. Then he arose, and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water: and they ceased, and there was a calm.
verses.bsb: The disciples went and woke Him, saying, “Master, Master, we are perishing!”  Then Jesus got up and rebuked the wind and the raging waters, and they subsided, and all was calm.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Where is your faith? And they being afraid wondered, saying one to another, What manner of man is this! for he commandeth even the winds and water, and they obey him.
verses.bsb: “Where is your faith?” He asked.  Frightened and amazed, they asked one another, “Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey Him!”

verses.kjv: ¶ And they arrived at the country of the Gadarenes, which is over against Galilee.
verses.bsb: Then they sailed to the region of the Gerasenes, across the lake from Galilee.

verses.kjv: And when he went forth to land, there met him out of the city a certain man, which had devils long time, and ware no clothes, neither abode in any house, but in the tombs.
verses.bsb: When Jesus stepped ashore, He was met by a demon-possessed man from the town. For a long time this man had not worn clothing or lived in a house, but he stayed in the tombs.

verses.kjv: When he saw Jesus, he cried out, and fell down before him, and with a loud voice said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God most high? I beseech thee, torment me not.
verses.bsb: When the man saw Jesus, he cried out and fell down before Him, shouting in a loud voice, “What do You want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg You not to torture me!”

verses.kjv: (For he had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For oftentimes it had caught him: and he was kept bound with chains and in fetters; and he brake the bands, and was driven of the devil into the wilderness.)
verses.bsb: For Jesus had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had seized him, and though he was bound with chains and shackles, he had broken the chains and been driven by the demon into solitary places.

verses.kjv: And Jesus asked him, saying, What is thy name? And he said, Legion: because many devils were entered into him.
verses.bsb: “What is your name?” Jesus asked.  “Legion,” he replied, because many demons had gone into him.

verses.kjv: And they besought him that he would not command them to go out into the deep.
verses.bsb: And the demons kept begging Jesus not to order them to go into the Abyss.

verses.kjv: And there was there an herd of many swine feeding on the mountain: and they besought him that he would suffer them to enter into them. And he suffered them.
verses.bsb: There on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding. So the demons begged Jesus to let them enter the pigs, and He gave them permission.

verses.kjv: Then went the devils out of the man, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the lake, and were choked.
verses.bsb: Then the demons came out of the man and went into the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and was drowned.

verses.kjv: When they that fed them saw what was done, they fled, and went and told it in the city and in the country.
verses.bsb: When those tending the pigs saw what had happened, they ran off and reported this in the town and countryside.

verses.kjv: Then they went out to see what was done; and came to Jesus, and found the man, out of whom the devils were departed, sitting at the feet of Jesus, clothed, and in his right mind: and they were afraid.
verses.bsb: So the people went out to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man whom the demons had left, sitting at Jesus’ feet, clothed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.

verses.kjv: They also which saw it told them by what means he that was possessed of the devils was healed.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, those who had seen it reported how the demon-possessed man had been healed.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then the whole multitude of the country of the Gadarenes round about besought him to depart from them; for they were taken with great fear: and he went up into the ship, and returned back again.
verses.bsb: Then all the people of the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to depart from them, because great fear had taken hold of them. So He got into the boat and started back.

verses.kjv: Now the man out of whom the devils were departed besought him that he might be with him: but Jesus sent him away, saying,
verses.bsb: The man whom the demons had left begged to go with Jesus. But He sent him away, saying,

verses.kjv: Return to thine own house, and shew how great things God hath done unto thee. And he went his way, and published throughout the whole city how great things Jesus had done unto him.
verses.bsb: “Return home and describe how much God has done for you.” So the man went away and proclaimed all over the town how much Jesus had done for him.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, when Jesus was returned, the people gladly received him: for they were all waiting for him.
verses.bsb: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed Him, for they had all been waiting for Him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, there came a man named Jairus, and he was a ruler of the synagogue: and he fell down at Jesus’ feet, and besought him that he would come into his house:
verses.bsb: Just then a synagogue leader named Jairus came and fell at Jesus’ feet. He begged Him to come to his house,

verses.kjv: For he had one only daughter, about twelve years of age, and she lay a dying. But as he went the people thronged him.
verses.bsb: because his only daughter, who was about twelve, was dying.  As Jesus went with him, the crowds pressed around Him,

verses.kjv: ¶ And a woman having an issue of blood twelve years, which had spent all her living upon physicians, neither could be healed of any,
verses.bsb: including a woman who had suffered from bleeding for twelve years. She had spent all her money on physicians, but no one was able to heal her.

verses.kjv: Came behind him, and touched the border of his garment: and immediately her issue of blood stanched.
verses.bsb: She came up behind Jesus and touched the fringe of His cloak, and immediately her bleeding stopped.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said, Who touched me? When all denied, Peter and they that were with him said, Master, the multitude throng thee and press thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?
verses.bsb: “Who touched Me?” Jesus asked.  But they all denied it. “Master,” said Peter, “the people are crowding and pressing against You.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said, Somebody hath touched me: for I perceive that virtue is gone out of me.
verses.bsb: But Jesus declared, “Someone touched Me, for I know that power has gone out from Me.”

verses.kjv: And when the woman saw that she was not hid, she came trembling, and falling down before him, she declared unto him before all the people for what cause she had touched him, and how she was healed immediately.
verses.bsb: Then the woman, seeing that she could not escape notice, came trembling and fell down before Him. In the presence of all the people, she explained why she had touched Him and how she had immediately been healed.

verses.kjv: And he said unto her, Daughter, be of good comfort: thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace.
verses.bsb: “Daughter,” said Jesus, “your faith has healed you. Go in peace.”

verses.kjv: ¶ While he yet spake, there cometh one from the ruler of the synagogue’s house, saying to him, Thy daughter is dead; trouble not the Master.
verses.bsb: While He was still speaking, someone arrived from the house of the synagogue leader. “Your daughter is dead,” he told Jairus. “Do not bother the Teacher anymore.”

verses.kjv: But when Jesus heard it, he answered him, saying, Fear not: believe only, and she shall be made whole.
verses.bsb: But Jesus overheard them and said to Jairus, “Do not be afraid; just believe, and she will be healed.”

verses.kjv: And when he came into the house, he suffered no man to go in, save Peter, and James, and John, and the father and the mother of the maiden.
verses.bsb: When He entered the house, He did not allow anyone to go in with Him except Peter, John, James, and the child’s father and mother.

verses.kjv: And all wept, and bewailed her: but he said, Weep not; she is not dead, but sleepeth.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, everyone was weeping and mourning for her. But Jesus said, “Stop weeping; she is not dead but asleep.”

verses.kjv: And they laughed him to scorn, knowing that she was dead.
verses.bsb: And they laughed at Him, knowing that she was dead.

verses.kjv: And he put them all out, and took her by the hand, and called, saying, Maid, arise.
verses.bsb: But Jesus took her by the hand and called out, “Child, get up!”

verses.kjv: And her spirit came again, and she arose straightway: and he commanded to give her meat.
verses.bsb: Her spirit returned, and at once she got up. And He directed that she be given something to eat.

verses.kjv: And her parents were astonished: but he charged them that they should tell no man what was done.
verses.bsb: Her parents were astounded, but Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened.

verses.kjv: THEN he called his twelve disciples together, and gave them power and authority over all devils, and to cure diseases.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus called the Twelve together and gave them power and authority over all demons, and power to cure diseases.

verses.kjv: And he sent them to preach the kingdom of God, and to heal the sick.
verses.bsb: And He sent them out to proclaim the kingdom of God and to heal the sick.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Take nothing for your journey, neither staves, nor scrip, neither bread, neither money; neither have two coats apiece.
verses.bsb: “Take nothing for the journey,” He told them, “no staff, no bag, no bread, no money, no second tunic.

verses.kjv: And whatsoever house ye enter into, there abide, and thence depart.
verses.bsb: Whatever house you enter, stay there until you leave that area.

verses.kjv: And whosoever will not receive you, when ye go out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimony against them.
verses.bsb: If anyone does not welcome you, shake the dust off your feet when you leave that town, as a testimony against them.”

verses.kjv: And they departed, and went through the towns, preaching the gospel, and healing every where.
verses.bsb: So they set out and went from village to village, preaching the gospel and healing people everywhere.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by him: and he was perplexed, because that it was said of some, that John was risen from the dead;
verses.bsb: When Herod the tetrarch heard about all that was happening, he was perplexed. For some were saying that John had risen from the dead,

verses.kjv: And of some, that Elias had appeared; and of others, that one of the old prophets was risen again.
verses.bsb: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that a prophet of old had arisen.

verses.kjv: And Herod said, John have I beheaded: but who is this, of whom I hear such things? And he desired to see him.
verses.bsb: “I beheaded John,” Herod said, “but who is this man I hear such things about?” And he kept trying to see Jesus.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the apostles, when they were returned, told him all that they had done. And he took them, and went aside privately into a desert place belonging to the city called Bethsaida.
verses.bsb: Then the apostles returned and reported to Jesus all that they had done. Taking them away privately, He withdrew to a town called Bethsaida.

verses.kjv: And the people, when they knew it, followed him: and he received them, and spake unto them of the kingdom of God, and healed them that had need of healing.
verses.bsb: But the crowds found out and followed Him. He welcomed them and spoke to them about the kingdom of God, and He healed those who needed healing.

verses.kjv: And when the day began to wear away, then came the twelve, and said unto him, Send the multitude away, that they may go into the towns and country round about, and lodge, and get victuals: for we are here in a desert place.
verses.bsb: As the day neared its end, the Twelve came to Jesus and said, “Dismiss the crowd so they can go to the surrounding villages and countryside for lodging and provisions. For we are in a desolate place here.”

verses.kjv: But he said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they said, We have no more but five loaves and two fishes; except we should go and buy meat for all this people.
verses.bsb: But Jesus told them, “You give them something to eat.”  “We have only five loaves of bread and two fish,” they answered, “unless we go and buy food for all these people.”

verses.kjv: For they were about five thousand men. And he said to his disciples, Make them sit down by fifties in a company.
verses.bsb: (There were about five thousand men.)  He told His disciples, “Have them sit down in groups of about fifty each.”

verses.kjv: And they did so, and made them all sit down.
verses.bsb: They did so, and everyone was seated.

verses.kjv: Then he took the five loaves and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed them, and brake, and gave to the disciples to set before the multitude.
verses.bsb: Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, Jesus spoke a blessing and broke them. Then He gave them to the disciples to set before the people.

verses.kjv: And they did eat, and were all filled: and there was taken up of fragments that remained to them twelve baskets.
verses.bsb: They all ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as he was alone praying, his disciples were with him: and he asked them, saying, Whom say the people that I am?
verses.bsb: One day as Jesus was praying in private and the disciples were with Him, He questioned them: “Who do the crowds say I am?”

verses.kjv: They answering said, John the Baptist; but some say, Elias; and others say, that one of the old prophets is risen again.
verses.bsb: They replied, “Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, that a prophet of old has arisen.”

verses.kjv: He said unto them, But whom say ye that I am? Peter answering said, The Christ of God.
verses.bsb: “But what about you?” Jesus asked. “Who do you say I am?”  Peter answered, “The Christ of God.”

verses.kjv: And he straitly charged them, and commanded them to tell no man that thing;
verses.bsb: Jesus strictly warned them not to tell this to anyone.

verses.kjv: Saying, The Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be slain, and be raised the third day.
verses.bsb: “The Son of Man must suffer many things,” He said. “He must be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and He must be killed and on the third day be raised to life.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he said to them all, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow me.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to all of them, “If anyone wants to come after Me, he must deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow Me.

verses.kjv: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: but whosoever will lose his life for my sake, the same shall save it.
verses.bsb: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake will save it.

verses.kjv: For what is a man advantaged, if he gain the whole world, and lose himself, or be cast away?
verses.bsb: What does it profit a man to gain the whole world, yet lose or forfeit his very self?

verses.kjv: For whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words, of him shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he shall come in his own glory, and in his Father’s, and of the holy angels.
verses.bsb: If anyone is ashamed of Me and My words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of him when He comes in His glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.

verses.kjv: But I tell you of a truth, there be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: But I tell you truthfully, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the kingdom of God.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass about an eight days after these sayings, he took Peter and John and James, and went up into a mountain to pray.
verses.bsb: About eight days after Jesus had said these things, He took with Him Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.

verses.kjv: And as he prayed, the fashion of his countenance was altered, and his raiment was white and glistering.
verses.bsb: And as He was praying, the appearance of His face changed, and His clothes became radiantly white.

verses.kjv: And, behold, there talked with him two men, which were Moses and Elias:
verses.bsb: Suddenly two men, Moses and Elijah, began talking with Jesus.

verses.kjv: Who appeared in glory, and spake of his decease which he should accomplish at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: They appeared in glory and spoke about His departure, which He was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: But Peter and they that were with him were heavy with sleep: and when they were awake, they saw his glory, and the two men that stood with him.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile Peter and his companions were overcome by sleep, but when they awoke, they saw Jesus’ glory and the two men standing with Him.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as they departed from him, Peter said unto Jesus, Master, it is good for us to be here: and let us make three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias: not knowing what he said.
verses.bsb: As Moses and Elijah were leaving, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good for us to be here. Let us put up three shelters—one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.” (He did not know what he was saying.)

verses.kjv: While he thus spake, there came a cloud, and overshadowed them: and they feared as they entered into the cloud.
verses.bsb: While Peter was speaking, a cloud appeared and enveloped them, and they were afraid as they entered the cloud.

verses.kjv: And there came a voice out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloved Son: hear him.
verses.bsb: And a voice came from the cloud, saying, “This is My Son, whom I have chosen. Listen to Him!”

verses.kjv: And when the voice was past, Jesus was found alone. And they kept it close, and told no man in those days any of those things which they had seen.
verses.bsb: After the voice had spoken, only Jesus was present with them. The disciples kept this to themselves, and in those days they did not tell anyone what they had seen.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that on the next day, when they were come down from the hill, much people met him.
verses.bsb: The next day, when they came down from the mountain, Jesus was met by a large crowd.

verses.kjv: And, behold, a man of the company cried out, saying, Master, I beseech thee, look upon my son: for he is mine only child.
verses.bsb: Suddenly a man in the crowd cried out, “Teacher, I beg You to look at my son, for he is my only child.

verses.kjv: And, lo, a spirit taketh him, and he suddenly crieth out; and it teareth him that he foameth again, and bruising him hardly departeth from him.
verses.bsb: A spirit keeps seizing him, and he screams abruptly. It throws him into convulsions so that he foams at the mouth. It keeps mauling him and rarely departs from him.

verses.kjv: And I besought thy disciples to cast him out; and they could not.
verses.bsb: I begged Your disciples to drive it out, but they were unable.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said, O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you, and suffer you? Bring thy son hither.
verses.bsb: “O unbelieving and perverse generation!” Jesus replied. “How long must I remain with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.”

verses.kjv: And as he was yet a coming, the devil threw him down, and tare him. And Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the child, and delivered him again to his father.
verses.bsb: Even while the boy was approaching, the demon slammed him to the ground in a convulsion. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.

verses.kjv: ¶ And they were all amazed at the mighty power of God. But while they wondered every one at all things which Jesus did, he said unto his disciples,
verses.bsb: And they were all astonished at the greatness of God.  While everyone was marveling at all that Jesus was doing, He said to His disciples,

verses.kjv: Let these sayings sink down into your ears: for the Son of man shall be delivered into the hands of men.
verses.bsb: “Let these words sink into your ears: The Son of Man is about to be delivered into the hands of men.”

verses.kjv: But they understood not this saying, and it was hid from them, that they perceived it not: and they feared to ask him of that saying.
verses.bsb: But they did not understand this statement. It was veiled from them so that they could not comprehend it, and they were afraid to ask Him about it.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then there arose a reasoning among them, which of them should be greatest.
verses.bsb: Then an argument started among the disciples as to which of them would be the greatest.

verses.kjv: And Jesus, perceiving the thought of their heart, took a child, and set him by him,
verses.bsb: But Jesus, knowing the thoughts of their hearts, had a little child stand beside Him.

verses.kjv: And said unto them, Whosoever shall receive this child in my name receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me receiveth him that sent me: for he that is least among you all, the same shall be great.
verses.bsb: And He said to them, “Whoever welcomes this little child in My name welcomes Me, and whoever welcomes Me welcomes the One who sent Me. For whoever is the least among all of you, he is the greatest.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And John answered and said, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name; and we forbad him, because he followeth not with us.
verses.bsb: “Master,” said John, “we saw someone driving out demons in Your name, and we tried to stop him, because he does not accompany us.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Forbid him not: for he that is not against us is for us.
verses.bsb: “Do not stop him,” Jesus replied, “for whoever is not against you is for you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, when the time was come that he should be received up, he stedfastly set his face to go to Jerusalem,
verses.bsb: As the day of His ascension approached, Jesus resolutely set out for Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And sent messengers before his face: and they went, and entered into a village of the Samaritans, to make ready for him.
verses.bsb: He sent messengers on ahead, who went into a village of the Samaritans to make arrangements for Him.

verses.kjv: And they did not receive him, because his face was as though he would go to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: But the people there refused to welcome Him, because He was heading for Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And when his disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, wilt thou that we command fire to come down from heaven, and consume them, even as Elias did?
verses.bsb: When the disciples James and John saw this, they asked, “Lord, do You want us to call down fire from heaven to consume them?”

verses.kjv: But he turned, and rebuked them, and said, Ye know not what manner of spirit ye are of.
verses.bsb: But Jesus turned and rebuked them.

verses.kjv: For the Son of man is not come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them. And they went to another village.
verses.bsb: And He and His disciples went on to another village.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that, as they went in the way, a certain man said unto him, Lord, I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest.
verses.bsb: As they were walking along the road, someone said to Jesus, “I will follow You wherever You go.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Foxes have dens and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay His head.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto another, Follow me. But he said, Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.
verses.bsb: Then He said to another man, “Follow Me.”  The man replied, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”

verses.kjv: Jesus said unto him, Let the dead bury their dead: but go thou and preach the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: But Jesus told him, “Let the dead bury their own dead. You, however, go and proclaim the kingdom of God.”

verses.kjv: And another also said, Lord, I will follow thee; but let me first go bid them farewell, which are at home at my house.
verses.bsb: Still another said, “I will follow You, Lord; but first let me bid farewell to my family.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, No man, having put his hand to the plough, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus declared, “No one who puts his hand to the plow and then looks back is fit for the kingdom of God.”

verses.kjv: AFTER these things the Lord appointed other seventy also, and sent them two and two before his face into every city and place, whither he himself would come.
verses.bsb: After this, the Lord appointed seventy-two others and sent them two by two ahead of Him to every town and place He was about to visit.

verses.kjv: Therefore said he unto them, The harvest truly is great, but the labourers are few: pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he would send forth labourers into his harvest.
verses.bsb: And He told them, “The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into His harvest.

verses.kjv: Go your ways: behold, I send you forth as lambs among wolves.
verses.bsb: Go! I am sending you out like lambs among wolves.

verses.kjv: Carry neither purse, nor scrip, nor shoes: and salute no man by the way.
verses.bsb: Carry no purse or bag or sandals. Do not greet anyone along the road.

verses.kjv: And into whatsoever house ye enter, first say, Peace be to this house.
verses.bsb: Whatever house you enter, begin by saying, ‘Peace to this house.’

verses.kjv: And if the son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon it: if not, it shall turn to you again.
verses.bsb: If a man of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; if not, it will return to you.

verses.kjv: And in the same house remain, eating and drinking such things as they give: for the labourer is worthy of his hire. Go not from house to house.
verses.bsb: Stay at the same house, eating and drinking whatever you are offered. For the worker is worthy of his wages. Do not move around from house to house.

verses.kjv: And into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you, eat such things as are set before you:
verses.bsb: If you enter a town and they welcome you, eat whatever is set before you.

verses.kjv: And heal the sick that are therein, and say unto them, The kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.
verses.bsb: Heal the sick who are there and tell them, ‘The kingdom of God is near you.’

verses.kjv: But into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you not, go your ways out into the streets of the same, and say,
verses.bsb: But if you enter a town and they do not welcome you, go into the streets and declare,

verses.kjv: Even the very dust of your city, which cleaveth on us, we do wipe off against you: notwithstanding be ye sure of this, that the kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.
verses.bsb: ‘Even the dust of your town that clings to our feet, we wipe off as a testimony against you. Yet be sure of this: The kingdom of God is near.’

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, that it shall be more tolerable in that day for Sodom, than for that city.
verses.bsb: I tell you, it will be more bearable on that day for Sodom than for that town.

verses.kjv: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon, which have been done in you, they had a great while ago repented, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
verses.bsb: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

verses.kjv: But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment, than for you.
verses.bsb: But it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.

verses.kjv: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted to heaven, shalt be thrust down to hell.
verses.bsb: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades!

verses.kjv: He that heareth you heareth me; and he that despiseth you despiseth me; and he that despiseth me despiseth him that sent me.
verses.bsb: Whoever listens to you listens to Me; whoever rejects you rejects Me; and whoever rejects Me rejects the One who sent Me.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the seventy returned again with joy, saying, Lord, even the devils are subject unto us through thy name.
verses.bsb: The seventy-two returned with joy and said, “Lord, even the demons submit to us in Your name.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven.
verses.bsb: So He told them, “I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.

verses.kjv: Behold, I give unto you power to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy: and nothing shall by any means hurt you.
verses.bsb: Behold, I have given you authority to tread on snakes and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”

verses.kjv: ¶ In that hour Jesus rejoiced in spirit, and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes: even so, Father; for so it seemed good in thy sight.
verses.bsb: At that time Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and declared, “I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.

verses.kjv: All things are delivered to me of my Father: and no man knoweth who the Son is, but the Father; and who the Father is, but the Son, and he to whom the Son will reveal him.
verses.bsb: All things have been entrusted to Me by My Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and no one knows who the Father is except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he turned him unto his disciples, and said privately, Blessed are the eyes which see the things that ye see:
verses.bsb: Then Jesus turned to the disciples and said privately, “Blessed are the eyes that see what you see.

verses.kjv: For I tell you, that many prophets and kings have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.
verses.bsb: For I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, a certain lawyer stood up, and tempted him, saying, Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
verses.bsb: One day an expert in the law stood up to test Him. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to inherit eternal life?”

verses.kjv: He said unto him, What is written in the law? how readest thou?
verses.bsb: “What is written in the Law?” Jesus replied. “How do you read it?”

verses.kjv: And he answering said, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbour as thyself.
verses.bsb: He answered, “‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind’ and ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’”

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Thou hast answered right: this do, and thou shalt live.
verses.bsb: “You have answered correctly,” Jesus said. “Do this and you will live.”

verses.kjv: But he, willing to justify himself, said unto Jesus, And who is my neighbour?
verses.bsb: But wanting to justify himself, he asked Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said, A certain man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among thieves, which stripped him of his raiment, and wounded him, and departed, leaving him half dead.
verses.bsb: Jesus took up this question and said, “A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho when he fell into the hands of robbers. They stripped him, beat him, and went away, leaving him half dead.

verses.kjv: And by chance there came down a certain priest that way: and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
verses.bsb: Now by chance a priest was going down the same road, but when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.

verses.kjv: And likewise a Levite, when he was at the place, came and looked on him, and passed by on the other side.
verses.bsb: So too, when a Levite came to that spot and saw him, he passed by on the other side.

verses.kjv: But a certain Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was: and when he saw him, he had compassion on him,
verses.bsb: But when a Samaritan on a journey came upon him, he looked at him and had compassion.

verses.kjv: And went to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring in oil and wine, and set him on his own beast, and brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
verses.bsb: He went to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. Then he put him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.

verses.kjv: And on the morrow when he departed, he took out two pence, and gave them to the host, and said unto him, Take care of him; and whatsoever thou spendest more, when I come again, I will repay thee.
verses.bsb: The next day he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper. ‘Take care of him,’ he said, ‘and on my return I will repay you for any additional expense.’

verses.kjv: Which now of these three, thinkest thou, was neighbour unto him that fell among the thieves?
verses.bsb: Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of robbers?”

verses.kjv: And he said, He that shewed mercy on him. Then said Jesus unto him, Go, and do thou likewise.
verses.bsb: “The one who showed him mercy,” replied the expert in the law.  Then Jesus told him, “Go and do likewise.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Now it came to pass, as they went, that he entered into a certain village: and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.
verses.bsb: As they traveled along, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed Him into her home.

verses.kjv: And she had a sister called Mary, which also sat at Jesus’ feet, and heard his word.
verses.bsb: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lord’s feet listening to His message.

verses.kjv: But Martha was cumbered about much serving, and came to him, and said, Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone? bid her therefore that she help me.
verses.bsb: But Martha was distracted by all the preparations to be made. She came to Jesus and said, “Lord, do You not care that my sister has left me to serve alone? Tell her to help me!”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto her, Martha, Martha, thou art careful and troubled about many things:
verses.bsb: “Martha, Martha,” the Lord replied, “you are worried and upset about many things.

verses.kjv: But one thing is needful: and Mary hath chosen that good part, which shall not be taken away from her.
verses.bsb: But only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the good portion, and it will not be taken away from her.”

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, that, as he was praying in a certain place, when he ceased, one of his disciples said unto him, Lord, teach us to pray, as John also taught his disciples.
verses.bsb: One day in a place where Jesus had just finished praying, one of His disciples requested, “Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, When ye pray, say, Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth.
verses.bsb: So Jesus told them, “When you pray, say:  ‘Father, hallowed be Your name. Your kingdom come.

verses.kjv: Give us day by day our daily bread.
verses.bsb: Give us each day our daily bread.

verses.kjv: And forgive us our sins; for we also forgive every one that is indebted to us. And lead us not into temptation; but deliver us from evil.
verses.bsb: And forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who sins against us. And lead us not into temptation.’”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go unto him at midnight, and say unto him, Friend, lend me three loaves;
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to them, “Suppose one of you goes to his friend at midnight and says, ‘Friend, lend me three loaves of bread,

verses.kjv: For a friend of mine in his journey is come to me, and I have nothing to set before him?
verses.bsb: because a friend of mine has come to me on a journey, and I have nothing to set before him.’

verses.kjv: And he from within shall answer and say, Trouble me not: the door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise and give thee.
verses.bsb: And suppose the one inside answers, ‘Do not bother me. My door is already shut, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you anything.’

verses.kjv: I say unto you, Though he will not rise and give him, because he is his friend, yet because of his importunity he will rise and give him as many as he needeth.
verses.bsb: I tell you, even though he will not get up to provide for him because of his friendship, yet because of the man’s persistence, he will get up and give him as much as he needs.

verses.kjv: And I say unto you, Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
verses.bsb: So I tell you: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened to you.

verses.kjv: For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.
verses.bsb: For everyone who asks receives; he who seeks finds; and to him who knocks, the door will be opened.

verses.kjv: If a son shall ask bread of any of you that is a father, will he give him a stone? or if he ask a fish, will he for a fish give him a serpent?
verses.bsb: What father among you, if his son asks for a fish, will give him a snake instead?

verses.kjv: Or if he shall ask an egg, will he offer him a scorpion?
verses.bsb: Or if he asks for an egg, will give him a scorpion?

verses.kjv: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children: how much more shall your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him?
verses.bsb: So if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give the Holy Spirit to those who ask Him!”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he was casting out a devil, and it was dumb. And it came to pass, when the devil was gone out, the dumb spake; and the people wondered.
verses.bsb: One day Jesus was driving out a demon that was mute. And when the demon was gone, the man who had been mute spoke. The crowds were amazed,

verses.kjv: But some of them said, He casteth out devils through Beelzebub the chief of the devils.
verses.bsb: but some of them said, “It is by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons, that He drives out demons.”

verses.kjv: And others, tempting him, sought of him a sign from heaven.
verses.bsb: And others tested Him by demanding a sign from heaven.

verses.kjv: But he, knowing their thoughts, said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and a house divided against a house falleth.
verses.bsb: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste, and a house divided against a house will fall.

verses.kjv: If Satan also be divided against himself, how shall his kingdom stand? because ye say that I cast out devils through Beelzebub.
verses.bsb: If Satan is divided against himself, how can his kingdom stand? After all, you say that I drive out demons by Beelzebul.

verses.kjv: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your sons cast them out? therefore shall they be your judges.
verses.bsb: And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons drive them out? So then, they will be your judges.

verses.kjv: But if I with the finger of God cast out devils, no doubt the kingdom of God is come upon you.
verses.bsb: But if I drive out demons by the finger of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.

verses.kjv: When a strong man armed keepeth his palace, his goods are in peace:
verses.bsb: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his house, his possessions are secure.

verses.kjv: But when a stronger than he shall come upon him, and overcome him, he taketh from him all his armour wherein he trusted, and divideth his spoils.
verses.bsb: But when someone stronger attacks and overpowers him, he takes away the armor in which the man trusted, and then he divides up his plunder.

verses.kjv: He that is not with me is against me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.
verses.bsb: He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters.

verses.kjv: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest; and finding none, he saith, I will return unto my house whence I came out.
verses.bsb: When an unclean spirit comes out of a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it. Then it says, ‘I will return to the house I left.’

verses.kjv: And when he cometh, he findeth it swept and garnished.
verses.bsb: On its return, it finds the house swept clean and put in order.

verses.kjv: Then goeth he, and taketh to him seven other spirits more wicked than himself; and they enter in, and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first.
verses.bsb: Then it goes and brings seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and dwell there. And the final plight of that man is worse than the first.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as he spake these things, a certain woman of the company lifted up her voice, and said unto him, Blessed is the womb that bare thee, and the paps which thou hast sucked.
verses.bsb: As Jesus was saying these things, a woman in the crowd raised her voice and said, “Blessed is the womb that bore You, and blessed are the breasts that nursed You!”

verses.kjv: But he said, Yea rather, blessed are they that hear the word of God, and keep it.
verses.bsb: But He replied, “Blessed rather are those who hear the word of God and obey it.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when the people were gathered thick together, he began to say, This is an evil generation: they seek a sign; and there shall no sign be given it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet.
verses.bsb: As the crowds were increasing, Jesus said, “This is a wicked generation. It demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of Jonah.

verses.kjv: For as Jonas was a sign unto the Ninevites, so shall also the Son of man be to this generation.
verses.bsb: For as Jonah was a sign to the Ninevites, so the Son of Man will be a sign to this generation.

verses.kjv: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and condemn them: for she came from the utmost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.
verses.bsb: The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and now One greater than Solomon is here.

verses.kjv: The men of Nineve shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.
verses.bsb: The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now One greater than Jonah is here.

verses.kjv: No man, when he hath lighted a candle, putteth it in a secret place, neither under a bushel, but on a candlestick, that they which come in may see the light.
verses.bsb: No one lights a lamp and puts it in a cellar or under a basket. Instead, he sets it on a stand, so those who enter can see the light.

verses.kjv: The light of the body is the eye: therefore when thine eye is single, thy whole body also is full of light; but when thine eye is evil, thy body also is full of darkness.
verses.bsb: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eyes are good, your whole body also is full of light. But when they are bad, your body is full of darkness.

verses.kjv: Take heed therefore that the light which is in thee be not darkness.
verses.bsb: Be careful, then, that the light within you is not darkness.

verses.kjv: If thy whole body therefore be full of light, having no part dark, the whole shall be full of light, as when the bright shining of a candle doth give thee light.
verses.bsb: So if your whole body is full of light, with no part of it in darkness, you will be radiant, as though a lamp were shining on you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And as he spake, a certain Pharisee besought him to dine with him: and he went in, and sat down to meat.
verses.bsb: As Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited Him to dine with him; so He went in and reclined at the table.

verses.kjv: And when the Pharisee saw it, he marvelled that he had not first washed before dinner.
verses.bsb: But the Pharisee was surprised to see that Jesus did not first wash before the meal.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto him, Now do ye Pharisees make clean the outside of the cup and the platter; but your inward part is full of ravening and wickedness.
verses.bsb: “Now then,” said the Lord, “you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside you are full of greed and wickedness.

verses.kjv: Ye fools, did not he that made that which is without make that which is within also?
verses.bsb: You fools! Did not the One who made the outside make the inside as well?

verses.kjv: But rather give alms of such things as ye have; and, behold, all things are clean unto you.
verses.bsb: But give as alms the things that are within you, and you will see that everything is clean for you.

verses.kjv: But woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye tithe mint and rue and all manner of herbs, and pass over judgment and the love of God: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.
verses.bsb: Woe to you Pharisees! You pay tithes of mint, rue, and every herb, but you disregard justice and the love of God. You should have practiced the latter without neglecting the former.

verses.kjv: Woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues, and greetings in the markets.
verses.bsb: Woe to you Pharisees! You love the chief seats in the synagogues and the greetings in the marketplaces.

verses.kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are as graves which appear not, and the men that walk over them are not aware of them.
verses.bsb: Woe to you! For you are like unmarked graves, which men walk over without even noticing.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then answered one of the lawyers, and said unto him, Master, thus saying thou reproachest us also.
verses.bsb: One of the experts in the law told Him, “Teacher, when You say these things, You insult us as well.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Woe unto you also, ye lawyers! for ye lade men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers.
verses.bsb: “Woe to you as well, experts in the law!” He replied. “You weigh men down with heavy burdens, but you yourselves will not lift a finger to lighten their load.

verses.kjv: Woe unto you! for ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.
verses.bsb: Woe to you! You build tombs for the prophets, but it was your fathers who killed them.

verses.kjv: Truly ye bear witness that ye allow the deeds of your fathers: for they indeed killed them, and ye build their sepulchres.
verses.bsb: So you are witnesses consenting to the deeds of your fathers: They killed the prophets, and you build their tombs.

verses.kjv: Therefore also said the wisdom of God, I will send them prophets and apostles, and some of them they shall slay and persecute:
verses.bsb: Because of this, the wisdom of God said, ‘I will send them prophets and apostles; some of them they will kill and others they will persecute.’

verses.kjv: That the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;
verses.bsb: As a result, this generation will be charged with the blood of all the prophets that has been shed since the foundation of the world,

verses.kjv: From the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharias, which perished between the altar and the temple: verily I say unto you, It shall be required of this generation.
verses.bsb: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who was killed between the altar and the sanctuary. Yes, I tell you, all of it will be charged to this generation.

verses.kjv: Woe unto you, lawyers! for ye have taken away the key of knowledge: ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.
verses.bsb: Woe to you experts in the law! For you have taken away the key to knowledge. You yourselves have not entered, and you have hindered those who were entering.”

verses.kjv: And as he said these things unto them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to urge him vehemently, and to provoke him to speak of many things:
verses.bsb: As Jesus went on from there, the scribes and Pharisees began to oppose Him bitterly and to ply Him with questions about many things,

verses.kjv: Laying wait for him, and seeking to catch something out of his mouth, that they might accuse him.
verses.bsb: waiting to catch Him in something He might say.

verses.kjv: IN the mean time, when there were gathered together an innumerable multitude of people, insomuch that they trode one upon another, he began to say unto his disciples first of all, Beware ye of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
verses.bsb: In the meantime, a crowd of many thousands had gathered, so that they were trampling one another. Jesus began to speak first to His disciples: “Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

verses.kjv: For there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; neither hid, that shall not be known.
verses.bsb: There is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed, and nothing hidden that will not be made known.

verses.kjv: Therefore whatsoever ye have spoken in darkness shall be heard in the light; and that which ye have spoken in the ear in closets shall be proclaimed upon the housetops.
verses.bsb: What you have spoken in the dark will be heard in the daylight, and what you have whispered in the inner rooms will be proclaimed from the housetops.

verses.kjv: And I say unto you my friends, Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.
verses.bsb: I tell you, My friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that can do no more.

verses.kjv: But I will forewarn you whom ye shall fear: Fear him, which after he hath killed hath power to cast into hell; yea, I say unto you, Fear him.
verses.bsb: But I will show you whom you should fear: Fear the One who, after you have been killed, has authority to throw you into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear Him!

verses.kjv: Are not five sparrows sold for two farthings, and not one of them is forgotten before God?
verses.bsb: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten by God.

verses.kjv: But even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore: ye are of more value than many sparrows.
verses.bsb: And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.

verses.kjv: Also I say unto you, Whosoever shall confess me before men, him shall the Son of man also confess before the angels of God:
verses.bsb: I tell you, everyone who confesses Me before men, the Son of Man will also confess him before the angels of God.

verses.kjv: But he that denieth me before men shall be denied before the angels of God.
verses.bsb: But whoever denies Me before men will be denied before the angels of God.

verses.kjv: And whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but unto him that blasphemeth against the Holy Ghost it shall not be forgiven.
verses.bsb: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

verses.kjv: And when they bring you unto the synagogues, and unto magistrates, and powers, take ye no thought how or what thing ye shall answer, or what ye shall say:
verses.bsb: When you are brought before synagogues, rulers, and authorities, do not worry about how to defend yourselves or what to say.

verses.kjv: For the Holy Ghost shall teach you in the same hour what ye ought to say.
verses.bsb: For at that time the Holy Spirit will teach you what you should say.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And one of the company said unto him, Master, speak to my brother, that he divide the inheritance with me.
verses.bsb: Someone in the crowd said to Him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Man, who made me a judge or a divider over you?
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “Man, who appointed Me judge or executor between you?”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Take heed, and beware of covetousness: for a man’s life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.
verses.bsb: And He said to them, “Watch out! Guard yourselves against every form of greed, for one’s life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.”

verses.kjv: And he spake a parable unto them, saying, The ground of a certain rich man brought forth plentifully:
verses.bsb: Then He told them a parable: “The ground of a certain rich man produced an abundance.

verses.kjv: And he thought within himself, saying, What shall I do, because I have no room where to bestow my fruits?
verses.bsb: So he thought to himself, ‘What shall I do, since I have nowhere to store my crops?’

verses.kjv: And he said, This will I do: I will pull down my barns, and build greater; and there will I bestow all my fruits and my goods.
verses.bsb: Then he said, ‘This is what I will do: I will tear down my barns and will build bigger ones, and there I will store up all my grain and my goods.

verses.kjv: And I will say to my soul, Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years; take thine ease, eat, drink, and be merry.
verses.bsb: Then I will say to myself, “You have plenty of good things laid up for many years. Take it easy. Eat, drink, and be merry!”’

verses.kjv: But God said unto him, Thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required of thee: then whose shall those things be, which thou hast provided?
verses.bsb: But God said to him, ‘You fool! This very night your life will be required of you. Then who will own what you have accumulated?’

verses.kjv: So is he that layeth up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.
verses.bsb: This is how it will be for anyone who stores up treasure for himself but is not rich toward God.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he said unto his disciples, Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat; neither for the body, what ye shall put on.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to His disciples, “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.

verses.kjv: The life is more than meat, and the body is more than raiment.
verses.bsb: For life is more than food, and the body more than clothes.

verses.kjv: Consider the ravens: for they neither sow nor reap; which neither have storehouse nor barn; and God feedeth them: how much more are ye better than the fowls?
verses.bsb: Consider the ravens: They do not sow or reap, they have no storehouse or barn; yet God feeds them. How much more valuable you are than the birds!

verses.kjv: And which of you with taking thought can add to his stature one cubit?
verses.bsb: Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?

verses.kjv: If ye then be not able to do that thing which is least, why take ye thought for the rest?
verses.bsb: So if you cannot do such a small thing, why do you worry about the rest?

verses.kjv: Consider the lilies how they grow: they toil not, they spin not; and yet I say unto you, that Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
verses.bsb: Consider how the lilies grow: They do not labor or spin. Yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory was adorned like one of these.

verses.kjv: If then God so clothe the grass, which is to day in the field, and to morrow is cast into the oven; how much more will he clothe you, O ye of little faith?
verses.bsb: If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, how much more will He clothe you, O you of little faith!

verses.kjv: And seek not ye what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink, neither be ye of doubtful mind.
verses.bsb: And do not be concerned about what you will eat or drink. Do not worry about it.

verses.kjv: For all these things do the nations of the world seek after: and your Father knoweth that ye have need of these things.
verses.bsb: For the Gentiles of the world strive after all these things, and your Father knows that you need them.

verses.kjv: ¶ But rather seek ye the kingdom of God; and all these things shall be added unto you.
verses.bsb: But seek His kingdom, and these things will be added unto you.

verses.kjv: Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the kingdom.
verses.bsb: Do not be afraid, little flock, for your Father is pleased to give you the kingdom.

verses.kjv: Sell that ye have, and give alms; provide yourselves bags which wax not old, a treasure in the heavens that faileth not, where no thief approacheth, neither moth corrupteth.
verses.bsb: Sell your possessions and give to the poor. Provide yourselves with purses that will not wear out, an inexhaustible treasure in heaven, where no thief approaches and no moth destroys.

verses.kjv: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
verses.bsb: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.

verses.kjv: Let your loins be girded about, and your lights burning;
verses.bsb: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps burning.

verses.kjv: And ye yourselves like unto men that wait for their lord, when he will return from the wedding; that when he cometh and knocketh, they may open unto him immediately.
verses.bsb: Then you will be like servants waiting for their master to return from the wedding banquet, so that when he comes and knocks, they can open the door for him at once.

verses.kjv: Blessed are those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching: verily I say unto you, that he shall gird himself, and make them to sit down to meat, and will come forth and serve them.
verses.bsb: Blessed are those servants whom the master finds on watch when he returns. Truly I tell you, he will dress himself to serve and will have them recline at the table, and he himself will come and wait on them.

verses.kjv: And if he shall come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those servants.
verses.bsb: Even if he comes in the second or third watch of the night and finds them alert, those servants will be blessed.

verses.kjv: And this know, that if the goodman of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched, and not have suffered his house to be broken through.
verses.bsb: But understand this: If the homeowner had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.

verses.kjv: Be ye therefore ready also: for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not.
verses.bsb: You also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour you do not expect.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Peter said unto him, Lord, speakest thou this parable unto us, or even to all?
verses.bsb: “Lord,” said Peter, “are You addressing this parable to us, or to everyone else as well?”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said, Who then is that faithful and wise steward, whom his lord shall make ruler over his household, to give them their portion of meat in due season?
verses.bsb: And the Lord answered, “Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom the master puts in charge of his servants to give them their portion at the proper time?

verses.kjv: Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.
verses.bsb: Blessed is that servant whose master finds him doing so when he returns.

verses.kjv: Of a truth I say unto you, that he will make him ruler over all that he hath.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.

verses.kjv: But and if that servant say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming; and shall begin to beat the menservants and maidens, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken;
verses.bsb: But suppose that servant says in his heart, ‘My master will be a long time in coming,’ and he begins to beat the menservants and maidservants, and to eat and drink and get drunk.

verses.kjv: The lord of that servant will come in a day when he looketh not for him, and at an hour when he is not aware, and will cut him in sunder, and will appoint him his portion with the unbelievers.
verses.bsb: The master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not anticipate. Then he will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers.

verses.kjv: And that servant, which knew his lord’s will, and prepared not himself, neither did according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes.
verses.bsb: That servant who knows his master’s will but does not get ready or follow his instructions will be beaten with many blows.

verses.kjv: But he that knew not, and did commit things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few stripes. For unto whomsoever much is given, of him shall be much required: and to whom men have committed much, of him they will ask the more.
verses.bsb: But the one who unknowingly does things worthy of punishment will be beaten with few blows. From everyone who has been given much, much will be required; and from him who has been entrusted with much, even more will be demanded.

verses.kjv: ¶ I am come to send fire on the earth; and what will I, if it be already kindled?
verses.bsb: I have come to ignite a fire on the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled!

verses.kjv: But I have a baptism to be baptized with; and how am I straitened till it be accomplished!
