verses.bsb: But I have a baptism to undergo, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!

verses.kjv: Suppose ye that I am come to give peace on earth? I tell you, Nay; but rather division:
verses.bsb: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, but division.

verses.kjv: For from henceforth there shall be five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.
verses.bsb: From now on, five in one household will be divided, three against two and two against three.

verses.kjv: The father shall be divided against the son, and the son against the father; the mother against the daughter, and the daughter against the mother; the mother in law against her daughter in law, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.
verses.bsb: They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he said also to the people, When ye see a cloud rise out of the west, straightway ye say, There cometh a shower; and so it is.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to the crowds, “As soon as you see a cloud rising in the west, you say, ‘A shower is coming,’ and that is what happens.

verses.kjv: And when ye see the south wind blow, ye say, There will be heat; and it cometh to pass.
verses.bsb: And when the south wind blows, you say, ‘It will be hot,’ and it is.

verses.kjv: Ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky and of the earth; but how is it that ye do not discern this time?
verses.bsb: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and sky. Why don’t you know how to interpret the present time?

verses.kjv: Yea, and why even of yourselves judge ye not what is right?
verses.bsb: And why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?

verses.kjv: ¶ When thou goest with thine adversary to the magistrate, as thou art in the way, give diligence that thou mayest be delivered from him; lest he hale thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and the officer cast thee into prison.
verses.bsb: Make every effort to reconcile with your adversary while you are on your way to the magistrate. Otherwise, he may drag you off to the judge, and the judge may hand you over to the officer, and the officer may throw you into prison.

verses.kjv: I tell thee, thou shalt not depart thence, till thou hast paid the very last mite.
verses.bsb: I tell you, you will not get out until you have paid the very last penny.”

verses.kjv: THERE were present at that season some that told him of the Galilæans, whose blood Pilate had mingled with their sacrifices.
verses.bsb: At that time some of those present told Jesus about the Galileans whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, Suppose ye that these Galilæans were sinners above all the Galilæans, because they suffered such things?
verses.bsb: To this He replied, “Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered this fate?

verses.kjv: I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.
verses.bsb: No, I tell you. But unless you repent, you too will all perish.

verses.kjv: Or those eighteen, upon whom the tower in Siloam fell, and slew them, think ye that they were sinners above all men that dwelt in Jerusalem?
verses.bsb: Or those eighteen who were killed when the tower of Siloam collapsed on them: Do you think that they were more sinful than all the others living in Jerusalem?

verses.kjv: I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.
verses.bsb: No, I tell you. But unless you repent, you too will all perish.”

verses.kjv: ¶ He spake also this parable; A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard; and he came and sought fruit thereon, and found none.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told this parable: “A man had a fig tree that was planted in his vineyard. He went to look for fruit on it, but did not find any.

verses.kjv: Then said he unto the dresser of his vineyard, Behold, these three years I come seeking fruit on this fig tree, and find none: cut it down; why cumbereth it the ground?
verses.bsb: So he said to the keeper of the vineyard, ‘Look, for the past three years I have come to search for fruit on this fig tree and haven’t found any. Therefore cut it down! Why should it use up the soil?’

verses.kjv: And he answering said unto him, Lord, let it alone this year also, till I shall dig about it, and dung it:
verses.bsb: ‘Sir,’ the man replied, ‘leave it alone again this year, until I dig around it and fertilize it.

verses.kjv: And if it bear fruit, well: and if not, then after that thou shalt cut it down.
verses.bsb: If it bears fruit next year, fine. But if not, you can cut it down.’”

verses.kjv: And he was teaching in one of the synagogues on the sabbath.
verses.bsb: One Sabbath Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues,

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, there was a woman which had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years, and was bowed together, and could in no wise lift up herself.
verses.bsb: and a woman there had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was hunched over and could not stand up straight.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus saw her, he called her to him, and said unto her, Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity.
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw her, He called her over and said, “Woman, you are set free from your disability.”

verses.kjv: And he laid his hands on her: and immediately she was made straight, and glorified God.
verses.bsb: Then He placed His hands on her, and immediately she straightened up and began to glorify God.

verses.kjv: And the ruler of the synagogue answered with indignation, because that Jesus had healed on the sabbath day, and said unto the people, There are six days in which men ought to work: in them therefore come and be healed, and not on the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: But the synagogue leader was indignant that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath. “There are six days for work,” he told the crowd. “So come and be healed on those days and not on the Sabbath.”

verses.kjv: The Lord then answered him, and said, Thou hypocrite, doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or his ass from the stall, and lead him away to watering?
verses.bsb: “You hypocrites!” the Lord replied. “Does not each of you on the Sabbath untie his ox or donkey from the stall and lead it to water?

verses.kjv: And ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan hath bound, lo, these eighteen years, be loosed from this bond on the sabbath day?
verses.bsb: Then should not this daughter of Abraham, whom Satan has kept bound for eighteen long years, be released from her bondage on the Sabbath day?”

verses.kjv: And when he had said these things, all his adversaries were ashamed: and all the people rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.
verses.bsb: When Jesus said this, all His adversaries were humiliated. And the whole crowd rejoiced at all the glorious things He was doing.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then said he, Unto what is the kingdom of God like? and whereunto shall I resemble it?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus asked, “What is the kingdom of God like? To what can I compare it?

verses.kjv: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and cast into his garden; and it grew, and waxed a great tree; and the fowls of the air lodged in the branches of it.
verses.bsb: It is like a mustard seed that a man tossed into his garden. It grew and became a tree, and the birds of the air nested in its branches.”

verses.kjv: And again he said, Whereunto shall I liken the kingdom of God?
verses.bsb: Again He asked, “To what can I compare the kingdom of God?

verses.kjv: It is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.
verses.bsb: It is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour, until all of it was leavened.”

verses.kjv: And he went through the cities and villages, teaching, and journeying toward Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus traveled throughout the towns and villages, teaching as He made His way toward Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: Then said one unto him, Lord, are there few that be saved? And he said unto them,
verses.bsb: “Lord,” someone asked Him, “will only a few people be saved?”  Jesus answered,

verses.kjv: ¶ Strive to enter in at the strait gate: for many, I say unto you, will seek to enter in, and shall not be able.
verses.bsb: “Make every effort to enter through the narrow door. For many, I tell you, will try to enter and will not be able.

verses.kjv: When once the master of the house is risen up, and hath shut to the door, and ye begin to stand without, and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, Lord, open unto us; and he shall answer and say unto you, I know you not whence ye are:
verses.bsb: After the master of the house gets up and shuts the door, you will stand outside knocking and saying, ‘Lord, open the door for us.’  But he will reply, ‘I do not know where you are from.’

verses.kjv: Then shall ye begin to say, We have eaten and drunk in thy presence, and thou hast taught in our streets.
verses.bsb: Then you will say, ‘We ate and drank with you, and you taught in our streets.’

verses.kjv: But he shall say, I tell you, I know you not whence ye are; depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity.
verses.bsb: And he will answer, ‘I tell you, I do not know where you are from. Depart from me, all you evildoers.’

verses.kjv: There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth, when ye shall see Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and all the prophets, in the kingdom of God, and you yourselves thrust out.
verses.bsb: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in the kingdom of God, but you yourselves are thrown out.

verses.kjv: And they shall come from the east, and from the west, and from the north, and from the south, and shall sit down in the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: People will come from east and west and north and south, and will recline at the table in the kingdom of God.

verses.kjv: And, behold, there are last which shall be first, and there are first which shall be last.
verses.bsb: And indeed, some who are last will be first, and some who are first will be last.”

verses.kjv: ¶ The same day there came certain of the Pharisees, saying unto him, Get thee out, and depart hence: for Herod will kill thee.
verses.bsb: At that very hour, some Pharisees came to Jesus and told Him, “Leave this place and get away, because Herod wants to kill You.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Go ye, and tell that fox, Behold, I cast out devils, and I do cures to day and to morrow, and the third day I shall be perfected.
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “Go tell that fox, ‘Look, I will keep driving out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will reach My goal.’

verses.kjv: Nevertheless I must walk to day, and to morrow, and the day following: for it cannot be that a prophet perish out of Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, I must keep going today and tomorrow and the next day, for it is not admissible for a prophet to perish outside of Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, which killest the prophets, and stonest them that are sent unto thee; how often would I have gathered thy children together, as a hen doth gather her brood under her wings, and ye would not!
verses.bsb: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those sent to her, how often I have longed to gather your children together as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you were unwilling!

verses.kjv: Behold, your house is left unto you desolate: and verily I say unto you, Ye shall not see me, until the time come when ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Look, your house is left to you desolate. And I tell you that you will not see Me again until you say, ‘Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord.’”

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, as he went into the house of one of the chief Pharisees to eat bread on the sabbath day, that they watched him.
verses.bsb: One Sabbath, Jesus went to eat in the home of a leading Pharisee, and those in attendance were watching Him closely.

verses.kjv: And, behold, there was a certain man before him which had the dropsy.
verses.bsb: Right there before Him was a man with dropsy.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering spake unto the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath day?
verses.bsb: So Jesus asked the experts in the law and the Pharisees, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath or not?”

verses.kjv: And they held their peace. And he took him, and healed him, and let him go;
verses.bsb: But they remained silent.  Then Jesus took hold of the man, healed him, and sent him on his way.

verses.kjv: And answered them, saying, Which of you shall have an ass or an ox fallen into a pit, and will not straightway pull him out on the sabbath day?
verses.bsb: And He asked them, “Which of you whose son or ox falls into a pit on the Sabbath day will not immediately pull him out?”

verses.kjv: And they could not answer him again to these things.
verses.bsb: And they were unable to answer these questions.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he put forth a parable to those which were bidden, when he marked how they chose out the chief rooms; saying unto them,
verses.bsb: When Jesus noticed how the guests chose the places of honor, He told them a parable:

verses.kjv: When thou art bidden of any man to a wedding, sit not down in the highest room; lest a more honourable man than thou be bidden of him;
verses.bsb: “When you are invited to a wedding banquet, do not sit in the place of honor, in case someone more distinguished than you has been invited.

verses.kjv: And he that bade thee and him come and say to thee, Give this man place; and thou begin with shame to take the lowest room.
verses.bsb: Then the host who invited both of you will come and tell you, ‘Give this man your seat.’ And in humiliation, you will have to take the last place.

verses.kjv: But when thou art bidden, go and sit down in the lowest room; that when he that bade thee cometh, he may say unto thee, Friend, go up higher: then shalt thou have worship in the presence of them that sit at meat with thee.
verses.bsb: But when you are invited, go and sit in the last place, so that your host will come and tell you, ‘Friend, move up to a better place.’ Then you will be honored in front of everyone at the table with you.

verses.kjv: For whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
verses.bsb: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and the one who humbles himself will be exalted.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then said he also to him that bade him, When thou makest a dinner or a supper, call not thy friends, nor thy brethren, neither thy kinsmen, nor thy rich neighbours; lest they also bid thee again, and a recompence be made thee.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited Him, “When you host a dinner or a banquet, do not invite your friends or brothers or relatives or rich neighbors. Otherwise, they may invite you in return, and you will be repaid.

verses.kjv: But when thou makest a feast, call the poor, the maimed, the lame, the blind:
verses.bsb: But when you host a banquet, invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, and the blind,

verses.kjv: And thou shalt be blessed; for they cannot recompense thee: for thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection of the just.
verses.bsb: and you will be blessed. Since they cannot repay you, you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when one of them that sat at meat with him heard these things, he said unto him, Blessed is he that shall eat bread in the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: When one of those reclining with Him heard this, he said to Jesus, “Blessed is everyone who will eat at the feast in the kingdom of God.”

verses.kjv: Then said he unto him, A certain man made a great supper, and bade many:
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “A certain man prepared a great banquet and invited many guests.

verses.kjv: And sent his servant at supper time to say to them that were bidden, Come; for all things are now ready.
verses.bsb: When it was time for the banquet, he sent his servant to tell those who had been invited, ‘Come, for everything is now ready.’

verses.kjv: And they all with one consent began to make excuse. The first said unto him, I have bought a piece of ground, and I must needs go and see it: I pray thee have me excused.
verses.bsb: But one after another they all began to make excuses. The first one said, ‘I have bought a field, and I need to go see it. Please excuse me.’

verses.kjv: And another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I go to prove them: I pray thee have me excused.
verses.bsb: Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to try them out. Please excuse me.’

verses.kjv: And another said, I have married a wife, and therefore I cannot come.
verses.bsb: Still another said, ‘I have married a wife, so I cannot come.’

verses.kjv: So that servant came, and shewed his lord these things. Then the master of the house being angry said to his servant, Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in hither the poor, and the maimed, and the halt, and the blind.
verses.bsb: The servant returned and reported all this to his master. Then the owner of the house became angry and said to his servant, ‘Go out quickly into the streets and alleys of the city, and bring in the poor, the crippled, the blind, and the lame.’

verses.kjv: And the servant said, Lord, it is done as thou hast commanded, and yet there is room.
verses.bsb: ‘Sir,’ the servant replied, ‘what you ordered has been done, and there is still room.’

verses.kjv: And the lord said unto the servant, Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.
verses.bsb: So the master told his servant, ‘Go out to the highways and hedges and compel them to come in, so that my house will be full.

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, That none of those men which were bidden shall taste of my supper.
verses.bsb: For I tell you, not one of those men who were invited will taste my banquet.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And there went great multitudes with him: and he turned, and said unto them,
verses.bsb: Large crowds were now traveling with Jesus, and He turned and said to them,

verses.kjv: If any man come to me, and hate not his father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brethren, and sisters, yea, and his own life also, he cannot be my disciple.
verses.bsb: “If anyone comes to Me and does not hate his father and mother and wife and children and brothers and sisters—yes, even his own life—he cannot be My disciple.

verses.kjv: And whosoever doth not bear his cross, and come after me, cannot be my disciple.
verses.bsb: And whoever does not carry his cross and follow Me cannot be My disciple.

verses.kjv: For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient to finish it?
verses.bsb: Which of you, wishing to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost to see if he has the resources to complete it?

verses.kjv: Lest haply, after he hath laid the foundation, and is not able to finish it, all that behold it begin to mock him,
verses.bsb: Otherwise, if he lays the foundation and is unable to finish the work, everyone who sees it will ridicule him,

verses.kjv: Saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish.
verses.bsb: saying, ‘This man could not finish what he started to build.’

verses.kjv: Or what king, going to make war against another king, sitteth not down first, and consulteth whether he be able with ten thousand to meet him that cometh against him with twenty thousand?
verses.bsb: Or what king on his way to war with another king will not first sit down and consider whether he can engage with ten thousand men the one coming against him with twenty thousand?

verses.kjv: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sendeth an ambassage, and desireth conditions of peace.
verses.bsb: And if he is unable, he will send a delegation while the other king is still far off, to ask for terms of peace.

verses.kjv: So likewise, whosoever he be of you that forsaketh not all that he hath, he cannot be my disciple.
verses.bsb: In the same way, any one of you who does not give up everything he has cannot be My disciple.

verses.kjv: ¶ Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be seasoned?
verses.bsb: Salt is good, but if the salt loses its savor, with what will it be seasoned?

verses.kjv: It is neither fit for the land, nor yet for the dunghill; but men cast it out. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.bsb: It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile, and it is thrown out.  He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

verses.kjv: THEN drew near unto him all the publicans and sinners for to hear him.
verses.bsb: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around to listen to Jesus.

verses.kjv: And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
verses.bsb: So the Pharisees and scribes began to grumble: “This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he spake this parable unto them, saying,
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told them this parable:

verses.kjv: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?
verses.bsb: “What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the pasture and go after the one that is lost, until he finds it?

verses.kjv: And when he hath found it, he layeth it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
verses.bsb: And when he finds it, he joyfully puts it on his shoulders,

verses.kjv: And when he cometh home, he calleth together his friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.
verses.bsb: comes home, and calls together his friends and neighbors to tell them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my lost sheep!’

verses.kjv: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.
verses.bsb: In the same way, I tell you that there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous ones who do not need to repent.

verses.kjv: ¶ Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find it?
verses.bsb: Or what woman who has ten silver coins and loses one of them does not light a lamp, sweep her house, and search carefully until she finds it?

verses.kjv: And when she hath found it, she calleth her friends and her neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost.
verses.bsb: And when she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors to say, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my lost coin.’

verses.kjv: Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.
verses.bsb: In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of God’s angels over one sinner who repents.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he said, A certain man had two sons:
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said, “There was a man who had two sons.

verses.kjv: And the younger of them said to his father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth to me. And he divided unto them his living.
verses.bsb: The younger son said to him, ‘Father, give me my share of the estate.’ So he divided his property between them.

verses.kjv: And not many days after the younger son gathered all together, and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living.
verses.bsb: After a few days, the younger son got everything together and journeyed to a distant country, where he squandered his wealth in wild living.

verses.kjv: And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want.
verses.bsb: After he had spent all he had, a severe famine swept through that country, and he began to be in need.

verses.kjv: And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.
verses.bsb: So he went and hired himself out to a citizen of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed the pigs.

verses.kjv: And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him.
verses.bsb: He longed to fill his belly with the pods the pigs were eating, but no one would give him a thing.

verses.kjv: And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger!
verses.bsb: Finally he came to his senses and said, ‘How many of my father’s hired servants have plenty of food? But here I am, starving to death!

verses.kjv: I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee,
verses.bsb: I will get up and go back to my father and say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.

verses.kjv: And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.
verses.bsb: I am no longer worthy to be called your son. Make me like one of your hired servants.”’

verses.kjv: And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.
verses.bsb: So he got up and went to his father. But while he was still in the distance, his father saw him and was filled with compassion. He ran to his son, embraced him, and kissed him.

verses.kjv: And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son.
verses.bsb: The son declared, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’

verses.kjv: But the father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe, and put it on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet:
verses.bsb: But the father said to his servants, ‘Quick! Bring the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.

verses.kjv: And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill it; and let us eat, and be merry:
verses.bsb: Bring the fattened calf and kill it. Let us feast and celebrate.

verses.kjv: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.
verses.bsb: For this son of mine was dead and is alive again! He was lost and is found!’ So they began to celebrate.

verses.kjv: Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard musick and dancing.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile the older son was in the field, and as he approached the house, he heard music and dancing.

verses.kjv: And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant.
verses.bsb: So he called one of the servants and asked what was going on.

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound.
verses.bsb: ‘Your brother has returned,’ he said, ‘and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has him back safe and sound.’

verses.kjv: And he was angry, and would not go in: therefore came his father out, and intreated him.
verses.bsb: The older son became angry and refused to go in. So his father came out and pleaded with him.

verses.kjv: And he answering said to his father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandment: and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:
verses.bsb: But he answered his father, ‘Look, all these years I have served you and never disobeyed a commandment of yours. Yet you never gave me even a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.

verses.kjv: But as soon as this thy son was come, which hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.
verses.bsb: But when this son of yours returns from squandering your wealth with prostitutes, you kill the fattened calf for him!’

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine.
verses.bsb: ‘Son, you are always with me,’ the father said, ‘and all that is mine is yours.

verses.kjv: It was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.
verses.bsb: But it was fitting to celebrate and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.’”

verses.kjv: AND he said also unto his disciples, There was a certain rich man, which had a steward; and the same was accused unto him that he had wasted his goods.
verses.bsb: Jesus also said to His disciples, “There was a rich man whose manager was accused of wasting his possessions.

verses.kjv: And he called him, and said unto him, How is it that I hear this of thee? give an account of thy stewardship; for thou mayest be no longer steward.
verses.bsb: So he called him in to ask, ‘What is this I hear about you? Turn in an account of your management, for you cannot be manager any longer.’

verses.kjv: Then the steward said within himself, What shall I do? for my lord taketh away from me the stewardship: I cannot dig; to beg I am ashamed.
verses.bsb: The manager said to himself, ‘What shall I do, now that my master is taking away my position? I am too weak to dig and too ashamed to beg.

verses.kjv: I am resolved what to do, that, when I am put out of the stewardship, they may receive me into their houses.
verses.bsb: I know what I will do, so that after my removal from management, people will welcome me into their homes.’

verses.kjv: So he called every one of his lord’s debtors unto him, and said unto the first, How much owest thou unto my lord?
verses.bsb: And he called in each one of his master’s debtors. ‘How much do you owe my master?’ he asked the first.

verses.kjv: And he said, An hundred measures of oil. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and sit down quickly, and write fifty.
verses.bsb: ‘A hundred measures of olive oil,’ he answered.  ‘Take your bill,’ said the manager. ‘Sit down quickly, and write fifty.’

verses.kjv: Then said he to another, And how much owest thou? And he said, An hundred measures of wheat. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and write fourscore.
verses.bsb: Then he asked another, ‘And how much do you owe?’  ‘A hundred measures of wheat,’ he replied.  ‘Take your bill and write eighty,’ he told him.

verses.kjv: And the lord commended the unjust steward, because he had done wisely: for the children of this world are in their generation wiser than the children of light.
verses.bsb: The master commended the dishonest manager because he had acted shrewdly. For the sons of this age are more shrewd in dealing with their own kind than are the sons of light.

verses.kjv: And I say unto you, Make to yourselves friends of the mammon of unrighteousness; that, when ye fail, they may receive you into everlasting habitations.
verses.bsb: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves, so that when it is gone, they will welcome you into eternal dwellings.

verses.kjv: He that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much: and he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much.
verses.bsb: Whoever is faithful with very little will also be faithful with much, and whoever is dishonest with very little will also be dishonest with much.

verses.kjv: If therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?
verses.bsb: So if you have not been faithful with worldly wealth, who will entrust you with true riches?

verses.kjv: And if ye have not been faithful in that which is another man’s, who shall give you that which is your own?
verses.bsb: And if you have not been faithful with the belongings of another, who will give you belongings of your own?

verses.kjv: ¶ No servant can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.
verses.bsb: No servant can serve two masters. Either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.”

verses.kjv: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things: and they derided him.
verses.bsb: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, heard all of this and were scoffing at Jesus.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God.
verses.bsb: So He said to them, “You are the ones who justify yourselves before men, but God knows your hearts. For what is prized among men is detestable before God.

verses.kjv: The law and the prophets were until John: since that time the kingdom of God is preached, and every man presseth into it.
verses.bsb: The Law and the Prophets were proclaimed until John. Since that time, the gospel of the kingdom of God is being preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.

verses.kjv: And it is easier for heaven and earth to pass, than one tittle of the law to fail.
verses.bsb: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for a single stroke of a pen to drop out of the Law.

verses.kjv: Whosoever putteth away his wife, and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and whosoever marrieth her that is put away from her husband committeth adultery.
verses.bsb: Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery, and he who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.

verses.kjv: ¶ There was a certain rich man, which was clothed in purple and fine linen, and fared sumptuously every day:
verses.bsb: Now there was a rich man dressed in purple and fine linen, who lived each day in joyous splendor.

verses.kjv: And there was a certain beggar named Lazarus, which was laid at his gate, full of sores,
verses.bsb: And a beggar named Lazarus lay at his gate, covered with sores

verses.kjv: And desiring to be fed with the crumbs which fell from the rich man’s table: moreover the dogs came and licked his sores.
verses.bsb: and longing to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table. Even the dogs came and licked his sores.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that the beggar died, and was carried by the angels into Abraham’s bosom: the rich man also died, and was buried;
verses.bsb: One day the beggar died and was carried by the angels to Abraham’s side. And the rich man also died and was buried.

verses.kjv: And in hell he lift up his eyes, being in torments, and seeth Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.
verses.bsb: In Hades, where he was in torment, he looked up and saw Abraham from afar, with Lazarus by his side.

verses.kjv: And he cried and said, Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue; for I am tormented in this flame.
verses.bsb: So he cried out, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue. For I am in agony in this fire.’

verses.kjv: But Abraham said, Son, remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things, and likewise Lazarus evil things: but now he is comforted, and thou art tormented.
verses.bsb: But Abraham answered, ‘Child, remember that during your lifetime you received your good things, while Lazarus received bad things. But now he is comforted here, while you are in agony.

verses.kjv: And beside all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed: so that they which would pass from hence to you cannot; neither can they pass to us, that would come from thence.
verses.bsb: And besides all this, a great chasm has been fixed between us and you, so that even those who wish cannot cross from here to you, nor can anyone cross from there to us.’

verses.kjv: Then he said, I pray thee therefore, father, that thou wouldest send him to my father’s house:
verses.bsb: ‘Then I beg you, father,’ he said, ‘send Lazarus to my father’s house,

verses.kjv: For I have five brethren; that he may testify unto them, lest they also come into this place of torment.
verses.bsb: for I have five brothers. Let him warn them, so that they will not also end up in this place of torment.’

verses.kjv: Abraham saith unto him, They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.
verses.bsb: But Abraham replied, ‘They have Moses and the prophets; let your brothers listen to them.’

verses.kjv: And he said, Nay, father Abraham: but if one went unto them from the dead, they will repent.
verses.bsb: ‘No, father Abraham,’ he said, ‘but if someone is sent to them from the dead, they will repent.’

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, If they hear not Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded, though one rose from the dead.
verses.bsb: Then Abraham said to him, ‘If they do not listen to Moses and the prophets, they will not be persuaded even if someone rises from the dead.’”

verses.kjv: THEN said he unto the disciples, It is impossible but that offences will come: but woe unto him, through whom they come!
verses.bsb: Jesus said to His disciples, “It is inevitable that stumbling blocks will come, but woe to the one through whom they come!

verses.kjv: It were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he cast into the sea, than that he should offend one of these little ones.
verses.bsb: It would be better for him to have a millstone hung around his neck and to be thrown into the sea than to cause one of these little ones to stumble.

verses.kjv: ¶ Take heed to yourselves: If thy brother trespass against thee, rebuke him; and if he repent, forgive him.
verses.bsb: Watch yourselves. If your brother sins, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.

verses.kjv: And if he trespass against thee seven times in a day, and seven times in a day turn again to thee, saying, I repent; thou shalt forgive him.
verses.bsb: Even if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times returns to say, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive him.”

verses.kjv: And the apostles said unto the Lord, Increase our faith.
verses.bsb: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said, If ye had faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye might say unto this sycamine tree, Be thou plucked up by the root, and be thou planted in the sea; and it should obey you.
verses.bsb: And the Lord answered, “If you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you can say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea,’ and it will obey you.

verses.kjv: But which of you, having a servant plowing or feeding cattle, will say unto him by and by, when he is come from the field, Go and sit down to meat?
verses.bsb: Which of you whose servant comes in from plowing or shepherding in the field will say to him, ‘Come at once and sit down to eat’?

verses.kjv: And will not rather say unto him, Make ready wherewith I may sup, and gird thyself, and serve me, till I have eaten and drunken; and afterward thou shalt eat and drink?
verses.bsb: Instead, won’t he tell him, ‘Prepare my meal and dress yourself to serve me while I eat and drink; and afterward you may eat and drink’?

verses.kjv: Doth he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded him? I trow not.
verses.bsb: Does he thank the servant because he did what he was told?

verses.kjv: So likewise ye, when ye shall have done all those things which are commanded you, say, We are unprofitable servants: we have done that which was our duty to do.
verses.bsb: So you also, when you have done everything commanded of you, should say, ‘We are unworthy servants; we have only done our duty.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as he went to Jerusalem, that he passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.
verses.bsb: While Jesus was on His way to Jerusalem, He was passing between Samaria and Galilee.

verses.kjv: And as he entered into a certain village, there met him ten men that were lepers, which stood afar off:
verses.bsb: As He entered one of the villages, He was met by ten lepers. They stood at a distance

verses.kjv: And they lifted up their voices, and said, Jesus, Master, have mercy on us.
verses.bsb: and raised their voices, shouting, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”

verses.kjv: And when he saw them, he said unto them, Go shew yourselves unto the priests. And it came to pass, that, as they went, they were cleansed.
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw them, He said, “Go, show yourselves to the priests.” And as they were on their way, they were cleansed.

verses.kjv: And one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, and with a loud voice glorified God,
verses.bsb: When one of them saw that he was healed, he came back, praising God in a loud voice.

verses.kjv: And fell down on his face at his feet, giving him thanks: and he was a Samaritan.
verses.bsb: He fell facedown at Jesus’ feet in thanksgiving to Him—and he was a Samaritan.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said, Were there not ten cleansed? but where are the nine?
verses.bsb: “Were not all ten cleansed?” Jesus asked. “Where then are the other nine?

verses.kjv: There are not found that returned to give glory to God, save this stranger.
verses.bsb: Was no one found except this foreigner to return and give glory to God?”

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Arise, go thy way: thy faith hath made thee whole.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to him, “Rise and go; your faith has made you well!”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when he was demanded of the Pharisees, when the kingdom of God should come, he answered them and said, The kingdom of God cometh not with observation:
verses.bsb: When asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, Jesus replied, “The kingdom of God will not come with observable signs.

verses.kjv: Neither shall they say, Lo here! or, lo there! for, behold, the kingdom of God is within you.
verses.bsb: Nor will people say, ‘Look, here it is,’ or ‘There it is.’ For you see, the kingdom of God is in your midst.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto the disciples, The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see it.
verses.bsb: Then He said to the disciples, “The time is coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it.

verses.kjv: And they shall say to you, See here; or, see there: go not after them, nor follow them.
verses.bsb: People will tell you, ‘Look, there He is!’ or ‘Look, here He is!’ Do not go out or chase after them.

verses.kjv: For as the lightning, that lighteneth out of the one part under heaven, shineth unto the other part under heaven; so shall also the Son of man be in his day.
verses.bsb: For just as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other, so will be the Son of Man in His day.

verses.kjv: But first must he suffer many things, and be rejected of this generation.
verses.bsb: But first He must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.

verses.kjv: And as it was in the days of Noe, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of man.
verses.bsb: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so also will it be in the days of the Son of Man:

verses.kjv: They did eat, they drank, they married wives, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark, and the flood came, and destroyed them all.
verses.bsb: People were eating and drinking, marrying and being given in marriage, up to the day Noah entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.

verses.kjv: Likewise also as it was in the days of Lot; they did eat, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they builded;
verses.bsb: It was the same in the days of Lot: People were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building.

verses.kjv: But the same day that Lot went out of Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven, and destroyed them all.
verses.bsb: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.

verses.kjv: Even thus shall it be in the day when the Son of man is revealed.
verses.bsb: It will be just like that on the day the Son of Man is revealed.

verses.kjv: In that day, he which shall be upon the housetop, and his stuff in the house, let him not come down to take it away: and he that is in the field, let him likewise not return back.
verses.bsb: On that day, let no one on the housetop come down to retrieve his possessions. Likewise, let no one in the field return for anything he has left behind.

verses.kjv: Remember Lot’s wife.
verses.bsb: Remember Lot’s wife!

verses.kjv: Whosoever shall seek to save his life shall lose it; and whosoever shall lose his life shall preserve it.
verses.bsb: Whoever tries to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life will preserve it.

verses.kjv: I tell you, in that night there shall be two men in one bed; the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left.
verses.bsb: I tell you, on that night two people will be in one bed: one will be taken and the other left.

verses.kjv: Two women shall be grinding together; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
verses.bsb: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.”

verses.kjv: And they answered and said unto him, Where, Lord? And he said unto them, Wheresoever the body is, thither will the eagles be gathered together.
verses.bsb: “Where, Lord?” they asked.  Jesus answered, “Wherever there is a carcass, there the vultures will gather.”

verses.kjv: AND he spake a parable unto them to this end, that men ought always to pray, and not to faint;
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told them a parable about their need to pray at all times and not lose heart:

verses.kjv: Saying, There was in a city a judge, which feared not God, neither regarded man:
verses.bsb: “In a certain town there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected men.

verses.kjv: And there was a widow in that city; and she came unto him, saying, Avenge me of mine adversary.
verses.bsb: And there was a widow in that town who kept appealing to him, ‘Give me justice against my adversary.’

verses.kjv: And he would not for a while: but afterward he said within himself, Though I fear not God, nor regard man;
verses.bsb: For a while he refused, but later he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God nor respect men,

verses.kjv: Yet because this widow troubleth me, I will avenge her, lest by her continual coming she weary me.
verses.bsb: yet because this widow keeps pestering me, I will give her justice. Then she will stop wearing me out with her perpetual requests.’”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said, Hear what the unjust judge saith.
verses.bsb: And the Lord said, “Listen to the words of the unjust judge.

verses.kjv: And shall not God avenge his own elect, which cry day and night unto him, though he bear long with them?
verses.bsb: Will not God bring about justice for His elect who cry out to Him day and night? Will He continue to defer their help?

verses.kjv: I tell you that he will avenge them speedily. Nevertheless when the Son of man cometh, shall he find faith on the earth?
verses.bsb: I tell you, He will promptly carry out justice on their behalf. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will He find faith on earth?”

verses.kjv: And he spake this parable unto certain which trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and despised others:
verses.bsb: To some who trusted in their own righteousness and viewed others with contempt, He also told this parable:

verses.kjv: Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee, and the other a publican.
verses.bsb: “Two men went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.

verses.kjv: The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself, God, I thank thee, that I am not as other men are, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even as this publican.
verses.bsb: The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed, ‘God, I thank You that I am not like the other men—swindlers, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.

verses.kjv: I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I possess.
verses.bsb: I fast twice a week and pay tithes of all that I acquire.’

verses.kjv: And the publican, standing afar off, would not lift up so much as his eyes unto heaven, but smote upon his breast, saying, God be merciful to me a sinner.
verses.bsb: But the tax collector stood at a distance, unwilling even to lift up his eyes to heaven. Instead, he beat his breast and said, ‘God, have mercy on me, a sinner!’

verses.kjv: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other: for every one that exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
verses.bsb: I tell you, this man, rather than the Pharisee, went home justified. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but the one who humbles himself will be exalted.”

verses.kjv: And they brought unto him also infants, that he would touch them: but when his disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
verses.bsb: Now people were even bringing their babies to Jesus for Him to place His hands on them. And when the disciples saw this, they rebuked those who brought them.

verses.kjv: But Jesus called them unto him, and said, Suffer little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: But Jesus called the children to Him and said, “Let the little children come to Me, and do not hinder them! For the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.

verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child shall in no wise enter therein.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, anyone who does not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.”

verses.kjv: And a certain ruler asked him, saying, Good Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
verses.bsb: Then a certain ruler asked Him, “Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? none is good, save one, that is, God.
verses.bsb: “Why do you call Me good?” Jesus replied. “No one is good except God alone.

verses.kjv: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honour thy father and thy mother.
verses.bsb: You know the commandments: ‘Do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not bear false witness, honor your father and mother.’”

verses.kjv: And he said, All these have I kept from my youth up.
verses.bsb: “All these I have kept from my youth,” he said.

verses.kjv: Now when Jesus heard these things, he said unto him, Yet lackest thou one thing: sell all that thou hast, and distribute unto the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, Jesus told him, “You still lack one thing: Sell everything you own and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow Me.”

verses.kjv: And when he heard this, he was very sorrowful: for he was very rich.
verses.bsb: But when the ruler heard this, he became very sad, because he was extremely wealthy.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus saw that he was very sorrowful, he said, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!
verses.bsb: Seeing the man’s sadness, Jesus said, “How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!

verses.kjv: For it is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.”

verses.kjv: And they that heard it said, Who then can be saved?
verses.bsb: Those who heard this asked, “Who then can be saved?”

verses.kjv: And he said, The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.
verses.bsb: But Jesus said, “What is impossible with man is possible with God.”

verses.kjv: Then Peter said, Lo, we have left all, and followed thee.
verses.bsb: “Look,” said Peter, “we have left all we had to follow You.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children, for the kingdom of God’s sake,
verses.bsb: “Truly I tell you,” Jesus replied, “no one who has left home or wife or brothers or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of God

verses.kjv: Who shall not receive manifold more in this present time, and in the world to come life everlasting.
verses.bsb: will fail to receive many times more in this age—and in the age to come, eternal life.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then he took unto him the twelve, and said unto them, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and all things that are written by the prophets concerning the Son of man shall be accomplished.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus took the Twelve aside and said to them, “Look, we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything the prophets have written about the Son of Man will be fulfilled.

verses.kjv: For he shall be delivered unto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and spitefully entreated, and spitted on:
verses.bsb: He will be delivered over to the Gentiles and will be mocked and insulted and spit upon.

verses.kjv: And they shall scourge him, and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.
verses.bsb: They will flog Him and kill Him, and on the third day He will rise again.”

verses.kjv: And they understood none of these things: and this saying was hid from them, neither knew they the things which were spoken.
verses.bsb: But the disciples did not understand any of these things. The meaning was hidden from them, and they did not comprehend what He was saying.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that as he was come nigh unto Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the way side begging:
verses.bsb: As Jesus drew near to Jericho, a blind man was sitting beside the road, begging.

verses.kjv: And hearing the multitude pass by, he asked what it meant.
verses.bsb: When he heard the crowd going by, he asked what was happening.

verses.kjv: And they told him, that Jesus of Nazareth passeth by.
verses.bsb: “Jesus of Nazareth is passing by,” they told him.

verses.kjv: And he cried, saying, Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
verses.bsb: So he called out, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”

verses.kjv: And they which went before rebuked him, that he should hold his peace: but he cried so much the more, Thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
verses.bsb: Those who led the way admonished him to be silent, but he cried out all the louder, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”

verses.kjv: And Jesus stood, and commanded him to be brought unto him: and when he was come near, he asked him,
verses.bsb: Jesus stopped and directed that the man be brought to Him. When he had been brought near, Jesus asked him,

verses.kjv: Saying, What wilt thou that I shall do unto thee? And he said, Lord, that I may receive my sight.
verses.bsb: “What do you want Me to do for you?”  “Lord,” he said, “let me see again.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Receive thy sight: thy faith hath saved thee.
verses.bsb: “Receive your sight!” Jesus replied. “Your faith has healed you.”

verses.kjv: And immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God: and all the people, when they saw it, gave praise unto God.
verses.bsb: Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus, glorifying God. And all the people who saw this gave praise to God.

verses.kjv: AND Jesus entered and passed through Jericho.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through.

verses.kjv: And, behold, there was a man named Zacchæus, which was the chief among the publicans, and he was rich.
verses.bsb: And there was a man named Zacchaeus, a chief tax collector, who was very wealthy.

verses.kjv: And he sought to see Jesus who he was; and could not for the press, because he was little of stature.
verses.bsb: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but could not see over the crowd because he was small in stature.

verses.kjv: And he ran before, and climbed up into a sycomore tree to see him: for he was to pass that way.
verses.bsb: So he ran on ahead and climbed a sycamore tree to see Him, since Jesus was about to pass that way.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus came to the place, he looked up, and saw him, and said unto him, Zacchæus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.
verses.bsb: When Jesus came to that place, He looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down, for I must stay at your house today.”

verses.kjv: And he made haste, and came down, and received him joyfully.
verses.bsb: So Zacchaeus hurried down and welcomed Him joyfully.

verses.kjv: And when they saw it, they all murmured, saying, That he was gone to be guest with a man that is a sinner.
verses.bsb: And all who saw this began to grumble, saying, “He has gone to be the guest of a sinful man!”

verses.kjv: And Zacchæus stood, and said unto the Lord; Behold, Lord, the half of my goods I give to the poor; and if I have taken any thing from any man by false accusation, I restore him fourfold.
verses.bsb: But Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “Look, Lord, half of my possessions I give to the poor, and if I have cheated anyone, I will repay it fourfold.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, This day is salvation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to him, “Today salvation has come to this house, because this man too is a son of Abraham.

verses.kjv: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.
verses.bsb: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save the lost.”

verses.kjv: And as they heard these things, he added and spake a parable, because he was nigh to Jerusalem, and because they thought that the kingdom of God should immediately appear.
verses.bsb: While the people were listening to this, Jesus proceeded to tell them a parable, because He was near Jerusalem and they thought the kingdom of God would appear imminently.

verses.kjv: He said therefore, A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.
verses.bsb: So He said, “A man of noble birth went to a distant country to lay claim to his kingship and then return.

verses.kjv: And he called his ten servants, and delivered them ten pounds, and said unto them, Occupy till I come.
verses.bsb: Beforehand, he called ten of his servants and gave them ten minas. ‘Conduct business with this until I return,’ he said.

verses.kjv: But his citizens hated him, and sent a message after him, saying, We will not have this man to reign over us.
verses.bsb: But his subjects hated him and sent a delegation after him to say, ‘We do not want this man to rule over us.’

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that when he was returned, having received the kingdom, then he commanded these servants to be called unto him, to whom he had given the money, that he might know how much every man had gained by trading.
verses.bsb: When he returned from procuring his kingship, he summoned the servants to whom he had given the money, to find out what each one had earned.

verses.kjv: Then came the first, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained ten pounds.
verses.bsb: The first servant came forward and said, ‘Master, your mina has produced ten more minas.’

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Well, thou good servant: because thou hast been faithful in a very little, have thou authority over ten cities.
verses.bsb: His master replied, ‘Well done, good servant! Because you have been faithful in a very small matter, you shall have authority over ten cities.’

verses.kjv: And the second came, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained five pounds.
verses.bsb: The second servant came and said, ‘Master, your mina has made five minas.’

verses.kjv: And he said likewise to him, Be thou also over five cities.
verses.bsb: And to this one he said, ‘You shall have authority over five cities.’

verses.kjv: And another came, saying, Lord, behold, here is thy pound, which I have kept laid up in a napkin:
verses.bsb: Then another servant came and said, ‘Master, here is your mina, which I have laid away in a piece of cloth.

verses.kjv: For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou layedst not down, and reapest that thou didst not sow.
verses.bsb: For I was afraid of you, because you are a harsh man. You withdraw what you did not deposit and reap what you did not sow.’

verses.kjv: And he saith unto him, Out of thine own mouth will I judge thee, thou wicked servant. Thou knewest that I was an austere man, taking up that I laid not down, and reaping that I did not sow:
verses.bsb: His master replied, ‘You wicked servant, I will judge you by your own words. So you knew that I am a harsh man, withdrawing what I did not deposit and reaping what I did not sow?

verses.kjv: Wherefore then gavest not thou my money into the bank, that at my coming I might have required mine own with usury?
verses.bsb: Why then did you not deposit my money in the bank, and upon my return I could have collected it with interest?’

verses.kjv: And he said unto them that stood by, Take from him the pound, and give it to him that hath ten pounds.
verses.bsb: Then he told those standing by, ‘Take the mina from him and give it to the one who has ten minas.’

verses.kjv: (And they said unto him, Lord, he hath ten pounds.)
verses.bsb: ‘Master,’ they said, ‘he already has ten!’

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, That unto every one which hath shall be given; and from him that hath not, even that he hath shall be taken away from him.
verses.bsb: He replied, ‘I tell you that everyone who has will be given more; but the one who does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.

verses.kjv: But those mine enemies, which would not that I should reign over them, bring hither, and slay them before me.
verses.bsb: And these enemies of mine who were unwilling for me to rule over them, bring them here and slay them in front of me.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when he had thus spoken, he went before, ascending up to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had said this, He went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when he was come nigh to Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount called the mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,
verses.bsb: As He approached Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives, He sent out two of His disciples,

verses.kjv: Saying, Go ye into the village over against you; in the which at your entering ye shall find a colt tied, whereon yet never man sat: loose him, and bring him hither.
verses.bsb: saying, “Go into the village ahead of you, and as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there, on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it here.

verses.kjv: And if any man ask you, Why do ye loose him? thus shall ye say unto him, Because the Lord hath need of him.
verses.bsb: If anyone asks, ‘Why are you untying it?’ tell him, ‘The Lord needs it.’”

verses.kjv: And they that were sent went their way, and found even as he had said unto them.
verses.bsb: So those who were sent went out and found it just as Jesus had told them.

verses.kjv: And as they were loosing the colt, the owners thereof said unto them, Why loose ye the colt?
verses.bsb: As they were untying the colt, its owners asked, “Why are you untying the colt?”

verses.kjv: And they said, The Lord hath need of him.
verses.bsb: “The Lord needs it,” they answered.

verses.kjv: And they brought him to Jesus: and they cast their garments upon the colt, and they set Jesus thereon.
verses.bsb: Then they led the colt to Jesus, threw their cloaks over it, and put Jesus on it.

verses.kjv: And as he went, they spread their clothes in the way.
verses.bsb: As He rode along, the people spread their cloaks on the road.

verses.kjv: And when he was come nigh, even now at the descent of the mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works that they had seen;
verses.bsb: And as He approached the descent from the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of disciples began to praise God joyfully in a loud voice for all the miracles they had seen:

verses.kjv: Saying, Blessed be the King that cometh in the name of the Lord: peace in heaven, and glory in the highest.
verses.bsb: “Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord!”   “Peace in heaven and glory in the highest!”

verses.kjv: And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude said unto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.
verses.bsb: But some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to Him, “Teacher, rebuke Your disciples!”

verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto them, I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.
verses.bsb: “I tell you,” He answered, “if they remain silent, the very stones will cry out.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when he was come near, he beheld the city, and wept over it,
verses.bsb: As Jesus approached Jerusalem and saw the city, He wept over it

verses.kjv: Saying, If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things which belong unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes.
verses.bsb: and said, “If only you had known on this day what would bring you peace! But now it is hidden from your eyes.

verses.kjv: For the days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side,
verses.bsb: For the days will come upon you when your enemies will barricade you and surround you and hem you in on every side.

verses.kjv: And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.
verses.bsb: They will level you to the ground—you and the children within your walls. They will not leave one stone on another, because you did not recognize the time of your visitation from God.”

verses.kjv: And he went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold therein, and them that bought;
verses.bsb: Then Jesus entered the temple courts and began to drive out those who were selling there.

verses.kjv: Saying unto them, It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of thieves.
verses.bsb: He declared to them, “It is written: ‘My house will be a house of prayer.’ But you have made it ‘a den of robbers.’”

verses.kjv: And he taught daily in the temple. But the chief priests and the scribes and the chief of the people sought to destroy him,
verses.bsb: Jesus was teaching at the temple every day, but the chief priests, scribes, and leaders of the people were intent on killing Him.

verses.kjv: And could not find what they might do: for all the people were very attentive to hear him.
verses.bsb: Yet they could not find a way to do so, because all the people hung on His words.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, that on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon him with the elders,
verses.bsb: One day as Jesus was teaching the people in the temple courts and proclaiming the gospel, the chief priests and scribes, together with the elders, came up to Him.

verses.kjv: And spake unto him, saying, Tell us, by what authority doest thou these things? or who is he that gave thee this authority?
verses.bsb: “Tell us,” they said, “by what authority are You doing these things, and who gave You this authority?”

verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:
verses.bsb: “I will also ask you a question,” Jesus replied. “Tell Me:

verses.kjv: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?
verses.bsb: John’s baptism—was it from heaven, or from men?”

verses.kjv: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then believed ye him not?
verses.bsb: They deliberated among themselves and said, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ He will ask, ‘Why did you not believe him?’

verses.kjv: But and if we say, Of men; all the people will stone us: for they be persuaded that John was a prophet.
verses.bsb: But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us, for they are convinced that John was a prophet.”

verses.kjv: And they answered, that they could not tell whence it was.
verses.bsb: So they answered that they did not know where it was from.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
verses.bsb: And Jesus replied, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”

verses.kjv: Then began he to speak to the people this parable; A certain man planted a vineyard, and let it forth to husbandmen, and went into a far country for a long time.
verses.bsb: Then He proceeded to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard, rented it out to some tenants, and went away for a long time.

verses.kjv: And at the season he sent a servant to the husbandmen, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard: but the husbandmen beat him, and sent him away empty.
verses.bsb: At harvest time, he sent a servant to the tenants to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants beat the servant and sent him away empty-handed.

verses.kjv: And again he sent another servant: and they beat him also, and entreated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.
verses.bsb: So he sent another servant, but they beat him and treated him shamefully, sending him away empty-handed.

verses.kjv: And again he sent a third: and they wounded him also, and cast him out.
verses.bsb: Then he sent a third, but they wounded him and threw him out.

verses.kjv: Then said the lord of the vineyard, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: it may be they will reverence him when they see him.
verses.bsb: ‘What shall I do?’ asked the owner of the vineyard. ‘I will send my beloved son. Perhaps they will respect him.’

verses.kjv: But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir: come, let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.
verses.bsb: But when the tenants saw the son, they discussed it among themselves and said, ‘This is the heir. Let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’

verses.kjv: So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do unto them?
verses.bsb: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.  What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?

verses.kjv: He shall come and destroy these husbandmen, and shall give the vineyard to others. And when they heard it, they said, God forbid.
verses.bsb: He will come and kill those tenants, and will give the vineyard to others.”  And when the people heard this, they said, “May such a thing never happen!”

verses.kjv: And he beheld them, and said, What is this then that is written, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner?
verses.bsb: But Jesus looked directly at them and said, “Then what is the meaning of that which is written:  ‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone’?

verses.kjv: Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.
verses.bsb: Everyone who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, but he on whom it falls will be crushed.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the chief priests and the scribes the same hour sought to lay hands on him; and they feared the people: for they perceived that he had spoken this parable against them.
verses.bsb: When the scribes and chief priests realized that Jesus had spoken this parable against them, they sought to arrest Him that very hour. But they were afraid of the people.

verses.kjv: And they watched him, and sent forth spies, which should feign themselves just men, that they might take hold of his words, that so they might deliver him unto the power and authority of the governor.
verses.bsb: So they watched Him closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They were hoping to catch Him in His words in order to hand Him over to the rule and authority of the governor.

verses.kjv: And they asked him, saying, Master, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, neither acceptest thou the person of any, but teachest the way of God truly:
verses.bsb: “Teacher,” they inquired, “we know that You speak and teach correctly. You show no partiality, but teach the way of God in accordance with the truth.

verses.kjv: Is it lawful for us to give tribute unto Cæsar, or no?
verses.bsb: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”

verses.kjv: But he perceived their craftiness, and said unto them, Why tempt ye me?
verses.bsb: But Jesus saw through their duplicity and said to them,

verses.kjv: Shew me a penny. Whose image and superscription hath it? They answered and said, Cæsar’s.
verses.bsb: “Show Me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?”  “Caesar’s,” they answered.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Render therefore unto Cæsar the things which be Cæsar’s, and unto God the things which be God’s.
verses.bsb: So Jesus told them, “Give to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.”

verses.kjv: And they could not take hold of his words before the people: and they marvelled at his answer, and held their peace.
verses.bsb: And they were unable to trap Him in His words before the people; and amazed at His answer, they fell silent.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then came to him certain of the Sadducees, which deny that there is any resurrection; and they asked him,
verses.bsb: Then some of the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to question Him.

verses.kjv: Saying, Master, Moses wrote unto us, If any man’s brother die, having a wife, and he die without children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
verses.bsb: “Teacher,” they said, “Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man is to marry his brother’s widow and raise up offspring for him.

verses.kjv: There were therefore seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and died without children.
verses.bsb: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a wife, but died childless.

verses.kjv: And the second took her to wife, and he died childless.
verses.bsb: Then the second

verses.kjv: And the third took her; and in like manner the seven also: and they left no children, and died.
verses.bsb: and the third married the widow, and in the same way all seven died, leaving no children.

verses.kjv: Last of all the woman died also.
verses.bsb: And last of all, the woman died.

verses.kjv: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them is she? for seven had her to wife.
verses.bsb: So then, in the resurrection, whose wife will she be? For all seven were married to her.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, The children of this world marry, and are given in marriage:
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage.

verses.kjv: But they which shall be accounted worthy to obtain that world, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage:
verses.bsb: But those who are considered worthy to share in the age to come and in the resurrection from the dead will neither marry nor be given in marriage.

verses.kjv: Neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the angels; and are the children of God, being the children of the resurrection.
verses.bsb: In fact, they can no longer die, because they are like the angels. And since they are sons of the resurrection, they are sons of God.

verses.kjv: Now that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
verses.bsb: Even Moses demonstrates that the dead are raised, in the passage about the burning bush. For he calls the Lord ‘the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’

verses.kjv: For he is not a God of the dead, but of the living: for all live unto him.
verses.bsb: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for to Him all are alive.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then certain of the scribes answering said, Master, thou hast well said.
verses.bsb: Some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, You have spoken well!”

verses.kjv: And after that they durst not ask him any question at all.
verses.bsb: And they did not dare to question Him any further.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, How say they that Christ is David’s son?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus declared, “How can it be said that the Christ is the Son of David?

verses.kjv: And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
verses.bsb: For David himself says in the book of Psalms:  ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand

verses.kjv: Till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
verses.bsb: until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet.”’

verses.kjv: David therefore calleth him Lord, how is he then his son?
verses.bsb: Thus David calls Him ‘Lord.’ So how can He be David’s son?”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,
verses.bsb: In the hearing of all the people, Jesus said to His disciples,

verses.kjv: Beware of the scribes, which desire to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the markets, and the highest seats in the synagogues, and the chief rooms at feasts;
verses.bsb: “Beware of the scribes. They like to walk around in long robes, and they love the greetings in the marketplaces, the chief seats in the synagogues, and the places of honor at banquets.

verses.kjv: Which devour widows’ houses, and for a shew make long prayers: the same shall receive greater damnation.
verses.bsb: They defraud widows of their houses, and for a show make lengthy prayers. These men will receive greater condemnation.”

verses.kjv: AND he looked up, and saw the rich men casting their gifts into the treasury.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus looked up and saw the rich putting their gifts into the treasury,

verses.kjv: And he saw also a certain poor widow casting in thither two mites.
verses.bsb: and He saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins.

verses.kjv: And he said, Of a truth I say unto you, that this poor widow hath cast in more than they all:
verses.bsb: “Truly I tell you,” He said, “this poor widow has put in more than all the others.

verses.kjv: For all these have of their abundance cast in unto the offerings of God: but she of her penury hath cast in all the living that she had.
verses.bsb: For they all contributed out of their surplus, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on.”
