verses.kjv: ¶ And as some spake of the temple, how it was adorned with goodly stones and gifts, he said,
verses.bsb: As some of the disciples were remarking how the temple was adorned with beautiful stones and consecrated gifts, Jesus said,

verses.kjv: As for these things which ye behold, the days will come, in the which there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
verses.bsb: “As for what you see here, the time will come when not one stone will be left on another; every one will be thrown down.”

verses.kjv: And they asked him, saying, Master, but when shall these things be? and what sign will there be when these things shall come to pass?
verses.bsb: “Teacher,” they asked, “when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that they are about to take place?”

verses.kjv: And he said, Take heed that ye be not deceived: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and the time draweth near: go ye not therefore after them.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “See to it that you are not deceived. For many will come in My name, claiming, ‘I am He,’ and, ‘The time is near.’ Do not follow them.

verses.kjv: But when ye shall hear of wars and commotions, be not terrified: for these things must first come to pass; but the end is not by and by.
verses.bsb: When you hear of wars and rebellions, do not be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end is not imminent.”

verses.kjv: Then said he unto them, Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom:
verses.bsb: Then He told them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.

verses.kjv: And great earthquakes shall be in divers places, and famines, and pestilences; and fearful sights and great signs shall there be from heaven.
verses.bsb: There will be great earthquakes, famines, and pestilences in various places, along with fearful sights and great signs from heaven.

verses.kjv: But before all these, they shall lay their hands on you, and persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues, and into prisons, being brought before kings and rulers for my name’s sake.
verses.bsb: But before all this, they will seize you and persecute you. On account of My name they will deliver you to the synagogues and prisons, and they will bring you before kings and governors.

verses.kjv: And it shall turn to you for a testimony.
verses.bsb: This will be your opportunity to serve as witnesses.

verses.kjv: Settle it therefore in your hearts, not to meditate before what ye shall answer:
verses.bsb: So make up your mind not to worry beforehand how to defend yourselves.

verses.kjv: For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to gainsay nor resist.
verses.bsb: For I will give you speech and wisdom that none of your adversaries will be able to resist or contradict.

verses.kjv: And ye shall be betrayed both by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolks, and friends; and some of you shall they cause to be put to death.
verses.bsb: You will be betrayed even by parents and brothers and relatives and friends, and some of you will be put to death.

verses.kjv: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake.
verses.bsb: And you will be hated by everyone because of My name.

verses.kjv: But there shall not an hair of your head perish.
verses.bsb: Yet not even a hair of your head will perish.

verses.kjv: In your patience possess ye your souls.
verses.bsb: By your patient endurance you will gain your souls.

verses.kjv: And when ye shall see Jerusalem compassed with armies, then know that the desolation thereof is nigh.
verses.bsb: But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, you will know that her desolation is near.

verses.kjv: Then let them which are in Judæa flee to the mountains; and let them which are in the midst of it depart out; and let not them that are in the countries enter thereinto.
verses.bsb: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains, let those in the city get out, and let those in the country stay out of the city.

verses.kjv: For these be the days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.
verses.bsb: For these are the days of vengeance, to fulfill all that is written.

verses.kjv: But woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck, in those days! for there shall be great distress in the land, and wrath upon this people.
verses.bsb: How miserable those days will be for pregnant and nursing mothers! For there will be great distress upon the land and wrath against this people.

verses.kjv: And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led away captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.
verses.bsb: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be led captive into all the nations. And Jerusalem will be trodden down by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.

verses.kjv: ¶ And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with perplexity; the sea and the waves roaring;
verses.bsb: There will be signs in the sun and moon and stars, and on the earth dismay among the nations, bewildered by the roaring of the sea and the surging of the waves.

verses.kjv: Men’s hearts failing them for fear, and for looking after those things which are coming on the earth: for the powers of heaven shall be shaken.
verses.bsb: Men will faint from fear and anxiety over what is coming upon the earth, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

verses.kjv: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
verses.bsb: At that time they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

verses.kjv: And when these things begin to come to pass, then look up, and lift up your heads; for your redemption draweth nigh.
verses.bsb: When these things begin to happen, stand up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”

verses.kjv: And he spake to them a parable; Behold the fig tree, and all the trees;
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told them a parable: “Look at the fig tree and all the trees.

verses.kjv: When they now shoot forth, ye see and know of your own selves that summer is now nigh at hand.
verses.bsb: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.

verses.kjv: So likewise ye, when ye see these things come to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is nigh at hand.
verses.bsb: So also, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.

verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass away, till all be fulfilled.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have happened.

verses.kjv: Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.
verses.bsb: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will never pass away.

verses.kjv: ¶ And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and so that day come upon you unawares.
verses.bsb: But watch yourselves, or your hearts will be weighed down by dissipation, drunkenness, and the worries of life—and that day will spring upon you suddenly like a snare.

verses.kjv: For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.
verses.bsb: For it will come upon all who dwell on the face of all the earth.

verses.kjv: Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.
verses.bsb: So keep watch at all times, and pray that you may have the strength to escape all that is about to happen and to stand before the Son of Man.”

verses.kjv: And in the day time he was teaching in the temple; and at night he went out, and abode in the mount that is called the mount of Olives.
verses.bsb: Every day Jesus taught at the temple, but every evening He went out to spend the night on the Mount of Olives.

verses.kjv: And all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple, for to hear him.
verses.bsb: And early in the morning all the people would come to hear Him at the temple.

verses.kjv: NOW the feast of unleavened bread drew nigh, which is called the Passover.
verses.bsb: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread, called the Passover, was approaching,

verses.kjv: And the chief priests and scribes sought how they might kill him; for they feared the people.
verses.bsb: and the chief priests and scribes were looking for a way to put Jesus to death; for they feared the people.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then entered Satan into Judas surnamed Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve.
verses.bsb: Then Satan entered Judas Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.

verses.kjv: And he went his way, and communed with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray him unto them.
verses.bsb: And Judas went to discuss with the chief priests and temple officers how he might betray Jesus to them.

verses.kjv: And they were glad, and covenanted to give him money.
verses.bsb: They were delighted and agreed to give him money.

verses.kjv: And he promised, and sought opportunity to betray him unto them in the absence of the multitude.
verses.bsb: Judas consented, and began to look for an opportunity to betray Jesus to them in the absence of a crowd.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then came the day of unleavened bread, when the passover must be killed.
verses.bsb: Then came the day of Unleavened Bread on which the Passover lamb was to be sacrificed.

verses.kjv: And he sent Peter and John, saying, Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat.
verses.bsb: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover.”

verses.kjv: And they said unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare?
verses.bsb: “Where do You want us to prepare it?” they asked.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Behold, when ye are entered into the city, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he entereth in.
verses.bsb: He answered, “When you enter the city, a man carrying a jug of water will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters,

verses.kjv: And ye shall say unto the goodman of the house, The Master saith unto thee, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
verses.bsb: and say to the owner of that house, ‘The Teacher asks: Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with My disciples?’

verses.kjv: And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.
verses.bsb: And he will show you a large upper room, already furnished. Make preparations there.”

verses.kjv: And they went, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
verses.bsb: So they went and found it just as Jesus had told them. And they prepared the Passover.

verses.kjv: And when the hour was come, he sat down, and the twelve apostles with him.
verses.bsb: When the hour had come, Jesus reclined at the table with His apostles.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer:
verses.bsb: And He said to them, “I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before My suffering.

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: For I tell you that I will not eat it again until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.”

verses.kjv: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, Take this, and divide it among yourselves:
verses.bsb: After taking the cup, He gave thanks and said, “Take this and divide it among yourselves.

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.
verses.bsb: For I tell you that I will not drink of the fruit of the vine from now on until the kingdom of God comes.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he took bread, and gave thanks, and brake it, and gave unto them, saying, This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me.
verses.bsb: And He took the bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to them, saying, “This is My body, given for you; do this in remembrance of Me.”

verses.kjv: Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood, which is shed for you.
verses.bsb: In the same way, after supper He took the cup, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in My blood, which is poured out for you.

verses.kjv: ¶ But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table.
verses.bsb: Look! The hand of My betrayer is with Mine on the table.

verses.kjv: And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed!
verses.bsb: Indeed, the Son of Man will go as it has been determined, but woe to that man who betrays Him.”

verses.kjv: And they began to inquire among themselves, which of them it was that should do this thing.
verses.bsb: Then they began to question among themselves which of them was going to do this.

verses.kjv: ¶ And there was also a strife among them, which of them should be accounted the greatest.
verses.bsb: A dispute also arose among the disciples as to which of them would be considered the greatest.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors.
verses.bsb: So Jesus declared, “The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those in authority over them call themselves benefactors.

verses.kjv: But ye shall not be so: but he that is greatest among you, let him be as the younger; and he that is chief, as he that doth serve.
verses.bsb: But you shall not be like them. Instead, the greatest among you should be like the youngest, and the one who leads like the one who serves.

verses.kjv: For whether is greater, he that sitteth at meat, or he that serveth? is not he that sitteth at meat? but I am among you as he that serveth.
verses.bsb: For who is greater, the one who reclines at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one who reclines? But I am among you as one who serves.

verses.kjv: Ye are they which have continued with me in my temptations.
verses.bsb: You are the ones who have stood by Me in My trials.

verses.kjv: And I appoint unto you a kingdom, as my Father hath appointed unto me;
verses.bsb: And I bestow on you a kingdom, just as My Father has bestowed one on Me,

verses.kjv: That ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
verses.bsb: so that you may eat and drink at My table in My kingdom, and sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, behold, Satan hath desired to have you, that he may sift you as wheat:
verses.bsb: Simon, Simon, Satan has asked to sift each of you like wheat.

verses.kjv: But I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not: and when thou art converted, strengthen thy brethren.
verses.bsb: But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith will not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Lord, I am ready to go with thee, both into prison, and to death.
verses.bsb: “Lord,” said Peter, “I am ready to go with You even to prison and to death.”

verses.kjv: And he said, I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me.
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster will not crow today until you have denied three times that you know Me.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye any thing? And they said, Nothing.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus asked them, “When I sent you out without purse or bag or sandals, did you lack anything?”  “Nothing,” they answered.

verses.kjv: Then said he unto them, But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.
verses.bsb: “Now, however,” He told them, “the one with a purse should take it, and likewise a bag; and the one without a sword should sell his cloak and buy one.

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end.
verses.bsb: For I tell you that this Scripture must be fulfilled in Me: ‘And He was numbered with the transgressors.’ For what is written about Me is reaching its fulfillment.”

verses.kjv: And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And he said unto them, It is enough.
verses.bsb: So they said, “Look, Lord, here are two swords.”  “That is enough,” He answered.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he came out, and went, as he was wont, to the mount of Olives; and his disciples also followed him.
verses.bsb: Jesus went out as usual to the Mount of Olives, and the disciples followed Him.

verses.kjv: And when he was at the place, he said unto them, Pray that ye enter not into temptation.
verses.bsb: When He came to the place, He told them, “Pray that you will not enter into temptation.”

verses.kjv: And he was withdrawn from them about a stone’s cast, and kneeled down, and prayed,
verses.bsb: And He withdrew about a stone’s throw beyond them, where He knelt down and prayed,

verses.kjv: Saying, Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup from me: nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.
verses.bsb: “Father, if You are willing, take this cup from Me. Yet not My will, but Yours be done.”

verses.kjv: And there appeared an angel unto him from heaven, strengthening him.
verses.bsb: Then an angel from heaven appeared to Him and strengthened Him.

verses.kjv: And being in an agony he prayed more earnestly: and his sweat was as it were great drops of blood falling down to the ground.
verses.bsb: And in His anguish, He prayed more earnestly, and His sweat became like drops of blood falling to the ground.

verses.kjv: And when he rose up from prayer, and was come to his disciples, he found them sleeping for sorrow,
verses.bsb: When Jesus rose from prayer and returned to the disciples, He found them asleep, exhausted from sorrow.

verses.kjv: And said unto them, Why sleep ye? rise and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.
verses.bsb: “Why are you sleeping?” He asked. “Get up and pray so that you will not enter into temptation.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And while he yet spake, behold a multitude, and he that was called Judas, one of the twelve, went before them, and drew near unto Jesus to kiss him.
verses.bsb: While He was still speaking, a crowd arrived, led by the man called Judas, one of the Twelve. He approached Jesus to kiss Him.

verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto him, Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
verses.bsb: But Jesus asked him, “Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?”

verses.kjv: When they which were about him saw what would follow, they said unto him, Lord, shall we smite with the sword?
verses.bsb: Those around Jesus saw what was about to happen and said, “Lord, should we strike with our swords?”

verses.kjv: ¶ And one of them smote the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.
verses.bsb: And one of them struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said, Suffer ye thus far. And he touched his ear, and healed him.
verses.bsb: But Jesus answered, “No more of this!” And He touched the man’s ear and healed him.

verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto the chief priests, and captains of the temple, and the elders, which were come to him, Be ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and staves?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, temple officers, and elders who had come for Him, “Have you come out with swords and clubs as you would against an outlaw?

verses.kjv: When I was daily with you in the temple, ye stretched forth no hands against me: but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.
verses.bsb: Every day I was with you in the temple courts, and you did not lay a hand on Me. But this hour belongs to you and to the power of darkness.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then took they him, and led him, and brought him into the high priest’s house. And Peter followed afar off.
verses.bsb: Then they seized Jesus, led Him away, and took Him into the house of the high priest. And Peter followed at a distance.

verses.kjv: And when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the hall, and were set down together, Peter sat down among them.
verses.bsb: When those present had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat down among them.

verses.kjv: But a certain maid beheld him as he sat by the fire, and earnestly looked upon him, and said, This man was also with him.
verses.bsb: A servant girl saw him seated in the firelight and looked intently at him. “This man also was with Him,” she said.

verses.kjv: And he denied him, saying, Woman, I know him not.
verses.bsb: But Peter denied it. “Woman, I do not know Him,” he said.

verses.kjv: And after a little while another saw him, and said, Thou art also of them. And Peter said, Man, I am not.
verses.bsb: A short time later, someone else saw him and said, “You also are one of them.”  But Peter said, “Man, I am not.”

verses.kjv: And about the space of one hour after another confidently affirmed, saying, Of a truth this fellow also was with him: for he is a Galilæan.
verses.bsb: About an hour later, another man insisted, “Certainly this man was with Him, for he too is a Galilean.”

verses.kjv: And Peter said, Man, I know not what thou sayest. And immediately, while he yet spake, the cock crew.
verses.bsb: “Man, I do not know what you are talking about,” Peter replied.  While he was still speaking, the rooster crowed.

verses.kjv: And the Lord turned, and looked upon Peter. And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
verses.bsb: And the Lord turned and looked at Peter.  Then Peter remembered the word that the Lord had spoken to him: “Before the rooster crows today, you will deny Me three times.”

verses.kjv: And Peter went out, and wept bitterly.
verses.bsb: And he went outside and wept bitterly.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the men that held Jesus mocked him, and smote him.
verses.bsb: The men who were holding Jesus began to mock Him and beat Him.

verses.kjv: And when they had blindfolded him, they struck him on the face, and asked him, saying, Prophesy, who is it that smote thee?
verses.bsb: They blindfolded Him and kept demanding, “Prophesy! Who hit You?”

verses.kjv: And many other things blasphemously spake they against him.
verses.bsb: And they said many other blasphemous things against Him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And as soon as it was day, the elders of the people and the chief priests and the scribes came together, and led him into their council, saying,
verses.bsb: At daybreak the council of the elders of the people, both the chief priests and scribes, met together. They led Jesus into their Sanhedrin and said,

verses.kjv: Art thou the Christ? tell us. And he said unto them, If I tell you, ye will not believe:
verses.bsb: “If You are the Christ, tell us.”  Jesus answered, “If I tell you, you will not believe.

verses.kjv: And if I also ask you, ye will not answer me, nor let me go.
verses.bsb: And if I ask you a question, you will not answer.

verses.kjv: Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand of the power of God.
verses.bsb: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”

verses.kjv: Then said they all, Art thou then the Son of God? And he said unto them, Ye say that I am.
verses.bsb: So they all asked, “Are You then the Son of God?”  He replied, “You say that I am.”

verses.kjv: And they said, What need we any further witness? for we ourselves have heard of his own mouth.
verses.bsb: “Why do we need any more testimony?” they declared. “We have heard it for ourselves from His own lips.”

verses.kjv: AND the whole multitude of them arose, and led him unto Pilate.
verses.bsb: Then the whole council rose and led Jesus away to Pilate.

verses.kjv: And they began to accuse him, saying, We found this fellow perverting the nation, and forbidding to give tribute to Cæsar, saying that he himself is Christ a King.
verses.bsb: And they began to accuse Him, saying, “We found this man subverting our nation, forbidding payment of taxes to Caesar, and proclaiming Himself to be Christ, a King.”

verses.kjv: And Pilate asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answered him and said, Thou sayest it.
verses.bsb: So Pilate asked Him, “Are You the King of the Jews?”  “You have said so,” Jesus replied.

verses.kjv: Then said Pilate to the chief priests and to the people, I find no fault in this man.
verses.bsb: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, “I find no basis for a charge against this man.”

verses.kjv: And they were the more fierce, saying, He stirreth up the people, teaching throughout all Jewry, beginning from Galilee to this place.
verses.bsb: But they kept insisting, “He stirs up the people all over Judea with His teaching. He began in Galilee and has come all the way here.”

verses.kjv: When Pilate heard of Galilee, he asked whether the man were a Galilæan.
verses.bsb: When Pilate heard this, he asked if the man was a Galilean.

verses.kjv: And as soon as he knew that he belonged unto Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who himself also was at Jerusalem at that time.
verses.bsb: And learning that Jesus was under Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent Him to Herod, who himself was in Jerusalem at that time.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceeding glad: for he was desirous to see him of a long season, because he had heard many things of him; and he hoped to have seen some miracle done by him.
verses.bsb: When Herod saw Jesus, he was greatly pleased. He had wanted to see Him for a long time, because he had heard about Him and was hoping to see Him perform a miracle.

verses.kjv: Then he questioned with him in many words; but he answered him nothing.
verses.bsb: Herod questioned Jesus at great length, but He gave no answer.

verses.kjv: And the chief priests and scribes stood and vehemently accused him.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the chief priests and scribes stood there, vehemently accusing Him.

verses.kjv: And Herod with his men of war set him at nought, and mocked him, and arrayed him in a gorgeous robe, and sent him again to Pilate.
verses.bsb: And even Herod and his soldiers ridiculed and mocked Him. Dressing Him in a fine robe, they sent Him back to Pilate.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the same day Pilate and Herod were made friends together: for before they were at enmity between themselves.
verses.bsb: That day Herod and Pilate became friends; before this time they had been enemies.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Pilate, when he had called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,
verses.bsb: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,

verses.kjv: Said unto them, Ye have brought this man unto me, as one that perverteth the people: and, behold, I, having examined him before you, have found no fault in this man touching those things whereof ye accuse him:
verses.bsb: and said to them, “You brought me this man as one who was inciting the people to rebellion. I have examined Him here in your presence and found Him not guilty of your charges against Him.

verses.kjv: No, nor yet Herod: for I sent you to him; and, lo, nothing worthy of death is done unto him.
verses.bsb: Neither has Herod, for he sent Him back to us. As you can see, He has done nothing deserving of death.

verses.kjv: I will therefore chastise him, and release him.
verses.bsb: Therefore I will punish Him and release Him.”

verses.kjv: And they cried out all at once, saying, Away with this man, and release unto us Barabbas:
verses.bsb: But they all cried out in unison: “Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us!”

verses.kjv: (Who for a certain sedition made in the city, and for murder, was cast into prison.)
verses.bsb: (Barabbas had been imprisoned for an insurrection in the city, and for murder.)

verses.kjv: Pilate therefore, willing to release Jesus, spake again to them.
verses.bsb: Wanting to release Jesus, Pilate addressed them again,

verses.kjv: But they cried, saying, Crucify him, crucify him.
verses.bsb: but they kept shouting, “Crucify Him! Crucify Him!”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them the third time, Why, what evil hath he done? I have found no cause of death in him: I will therefore chastise him, and let him go.
verses.bsb: A third time he said to them, “What evil has this man done? I have found in Him no offense worthy of death. So after I punish Him, I will release Him.”

verses.kjv: And they were instant with loud voices, requiring that he might be crucified. And the voices of them and of the chief priests prevailed.
verses.bsb: But they were insistent, demanding with loud voices for Jesus to be crucified. And their clamor prevailed.

verses.kjv: And Pilate gave sentence that it should be as they required.
verses.bsb: So Pilate sentenced that their demand be met.

verses.kjv: And he released unto them him that for sedition and murder was cast into prison, whom they had desired; but he delivered Jesus to their will.
verses.bsb: As they had requested, he released the one imprisoned for insurrection and murder, and handed Jesus over to their will.

verses.kjv: And as they led him away, they laid hold upon one Simon, a Cyrenian, coming out of the country, and on him they laid the cross, that he might bear it after Jesus.
verses.bsb: As the soldiers led Him away, they seized Simon of Cyrene on his way in from the country, and put the cross on him to carry behind Jesus.

verses.kjv: ¶ And there followed him a great company of people, and of women, which also bewailed and lamented him.
verses.bsb: A great number of people followed Him, including women who kept mourning and wailing for Him.

verses.kjv: But Jesus turning unto them said, Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children.
verses.bsb: But Jesus turned to them and said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for Me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.

verses.kjv: For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck.
verses.bsb: Look, the days are coming when people will say, ‘Blessed are the barren women, the wombs that never bore, and breasts that never nursed!’

verses.kjv: Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us.
verses.bsb: At that time  ‘they will say to the mountains, “Fall on us!” and to the hills, “Cover us!”’

verses.kjv: For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?
verses.bsb: For if men do these things while the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry?”

verses.kjv: And there were also two other, malefactors, led with him to be put to death.
verses.bsb: Two others, who were criminals, were also led away to be executed with Jesus.

verses.kjv: And when they were come to the place, which is called Calvary, there they crucified him, and the malefactors, one on the right hand, and the other on the left.
verses.bsb: When they came to the place called The Skull, they crucified Him there, along with the criminals, one on His right and the other on His left.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then said Jesus, Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do. And they parted his raiment, and cast lots.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.” And they divided up His garments by casting lots.

verses.kjv: And the people stood beholding. And the rulers also with them derided him, saying, He saved others; let him save himself, if he be Christ, the chosen of God.
verses.bsb: The people stood watching, and the rulers sneered at Him, saying, “He saved others; let Him save Himself if He is the Christ of God, the Chosen One.”

verses.kjv: And the soldiers also mocked him, coming to him, and offering him vinegar,
verses.bsb: The soldiers also mocked Him and came up to offer Him sour wine.

verses.kjv: And saying, If thou be the king of the Jews, save thyself.
verses.bsb: “If You are the King of the Jews,” they said, “save Yourself!”

verses.kjv: And a superscription also was written over him in letters of Greek, and Latin, and Hebrew, THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
verses.bsb: Above Him was posted an inscription:  THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.

verses.kjv: ¶ And one of the malefactors which were hanged railed on him, saying, If thou be Christ, save thyself and us.
verses.bsb: One of the criminals who hung there heaped abuse on Him. “Are You not the Christ?” he said. “Save Yourself and us!”

verses.kjv: But the other answering rebuked him, saying, Dost not thou fear God, seeing thou art in the same condemnation?
verses.bsb: But the other one rebuked him, saying, “Do you not even fear God, since you are under the same judgment?

verses.kjv: And we indeed justly; for we receive the due reward of our deeds: but this man hath done nothing amiss.
verses.bsb: We are punished justly, for we are receiving what our actions deserve. But this man has done nothing wrong.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto Jesus, Lord, remember me when thou comest into thy kingdom.
verses.bsb: Then he said, “Jesus, remember me when You come into Your kingdom!”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, To day shalt thou be with me in paradise.
verses.bsb: And Jesus said to him, “Truly I tell you, today you will be with Me in Paradise.”

verses.kjv: And it was about the sixth hour, and there was a darkness over all the earth until the ninth hour.
verses.bsb: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over all the land until the ninth hour.

verses.kjv: And the sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was rent in the midst.
verses.bsb: The sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn down the middle.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when Jesus had cried with a loud voice, he said, Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit: and having said thus, he gave up the ghost.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus called out in a loud voice, “Father, into Your hands I commit My Spirit.” And when He had said this, He breathed His last.

verses.kjv: Now when the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, Certainly this was a righteous man.
verses.bsb: When the centurion saw what had happened, he gave glory to God, saying, “Surely this was a righteous man.”

verses.kjv: And all the people that came together to that sight, beholding the things which were done, smote their breasts, and returned.
verses.bsb: And when all the people who had gathered for this spectacle saw what had happened, they returned home beating their breasts.

verses.kjv: And all his acquaintance, and the women that followed him from Galilee, stood afar off, beholding these things.
verses.bsb: But all those who knew Jesus, including the women who had followed Him from Galilee, stood at a distance watching these things.

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, there was a man named Joseph, a counseller; and he was a good man, and a just:
verses.bsb: Now there was a Council member named Joseph, a good and righteous man,

verses.kjv: (The same had not consented to the counsel and deed of them;) he was of Arimathæa, a city of the Jews: who also himself waited for the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: who had not consented to their decision or action. He was from the Judean town of Arimathea, and was waiting for the kingdom of God.

verses.kjv: This man went unto Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus.
verses.bsb: He went to Pilate to ask for the body of Jesus.

verses.kjv: And he took it down, and wrapped it in linen, and laid it in a sepulchre that was hewn in stone, wherein never man before was laid.
verses.bsb: Then he took it down, wrapped it in a linen cloth, and placed it in a tomb cut into the rock, where no one had yet been laid.

verses.kjv: And that day was the preparation, and the sabbath drew on.
verses.bsb: It was Preparation Day, and the Sabbath was beginning.

verses.kjv: And the women also, which came with him from Galilee, followed after, and beheld the sepulchre, and how his body was laid.
verses.bsb: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed, and they saw the tomb and how His body was placed.

verses.kjv: And they returned, and prepared spices and ointments; and rested the sabbath day according to the commandment.
verses.bsb: Then they returned to prepare spices and perfumes. And they rested on the Sabbath, according to the commandment.

verses.kjv: NOW upon the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they came unto the sepulchre, bringing the spices which they had prepared, and certain others with them.
verses.bsb: On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, the women came to the tomb, bringing the spices they had prepared.

verses.kjv: And they found the stone rolled away from the sepulchre.
verses.bsb: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,

verses.kjv: And they entered in, and found not the body of the Lord Jesus.
verses.bsb: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as they were much perplexed thereabout, behold, two men stood by them in shining garments:
verses.bsb: While they were puzzling over this, suddenly two men in radiant apparel stood beside them.

verses.kjv: And as they were afraid, and bowed down their faces to the earth, they said unto them, Why seek ye the living among the dead?
verses.bsb: As the women bowed their faces to the ground in terror, the two men asked them, “Why do you look for the living among the dead?

verses.kjv: He is not here, but is risen: remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in Galilee,
verses.bsb: He is not here; He has risen! Remember how He told you while He was still in Galilee:

verses.kjv: Saying, The Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.
verses.bsb: ‘The Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and on the third day rise again.’”

verses.kjv: And they remembered his words,
verses.bsb: Then they remembered His words.

verses.kjv: And returned from the sepulchre, and told all these things unto the eleven, and to all the rest.
verses.bsb: And when they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the Eleven and to all the others.

verses.kjv: It was Mary Magdalene, and Joanna, and Mary the mother of James, and other women that were with them, which told these things unto the apostles.
verses.bsb: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told this to the apostles.

verses.kjv: And their words seemed to them as idle tales, and they believed them not.
verses.bsb: But their words seemed like nonsense to them, and they did not believe the women.

verses.kjv: Then arose Peter, and ran unto the sepulchre; and stooping down, he beheld the linen clothes laid by themselves, and departed, wondering in himself at that which was come to pass.
verses.bsb: Peter, however, got up and ran to the tomb. And after bending down and seeing only the linen cloths, he went away, wondering to himself what had happened.

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, two of them went that same day to a village called Emmaus, which was from Jerusalem about threescore furlongs.
verses.bsb: That same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
verses.bsb: They were talking with each other about everything that had happened.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, while they communed together and reasoned, Jesus himself drew near, and went with them.
verses.bsb: And as they talked and deliberated, Jesus Himself came up and walked along with them.

verses.kjv: But their eyes were holden that they should not know him.
verses.bsb: But their eyes were kept from recognizing Him.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?
verses.bsb: He asked them, “What are you discussing so intently as you walk along?”  They stood still, with sadness on their faces.

verses.kjv: And the one of them, whose name was Cleopas, answering said unto him, Art thou only a stranger in Jerusalem, and hast not known the things which are come to pass there in these days?
verses.bsb: One of them, named Cleopas, asked Him, “Are You the only visitor to Jerusalem who does not know the things that have happened there in recent days?”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What things? And they said unto him, Concerning Jesus of Nazareth, which was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people:
verses.bsb: “What things?” He asked.  “The events involving Jesus of Nazareth,” they answered. “This man was a prophet, powerful in speech and action before God and all the people.

verses.kjv: And how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him to be condemned to death, and have crucified him.
verses.bsb: Our chief priests and rulers delivered Him up to the sentence of death, and they crucified Him.

verses.kjv: But we trusted that it had been he which should have redeemed Israel: and beside all this, to day is the third day since these things were done.
verses.bsb: But we were hoping He was the One who would redeem Israel. And besides all this, it is the third day since these things took place.

verses.kjv: Yea, and certain women also of our company made us astonished, which were early at the sepulchre;
verses.bsb: Furthermore, some of our women astounded us. They were at the tomb early this morning,

verses.kjv: And when they found not his body, they came, saying, that they had also seen a vision of angels, which said that he was alive.
verses.bsb: but they did not find His body. They came and told us they had seen a vision of angels, who said that Jesus was alive.

verses.kjv: And certain of them which were with us went to the sepulchre, and found it even so as the women had said: but him they saw not.
verses.bsb: Then some of our companions went to the tomb and found it just as the women had described. But Him they did not see.”

verses.kjv: Then he said unto them, O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken:
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to them, “O foolish ones, how slow are your hearts to believe all that the prophets have spoken!

verses.kjv: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?
verses.bsb: Was it not necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then to enter His glory?”

verses.kjv: And beginning at Moses and all the prophets, he expounded unto them in all the scriptures the things concerning himself.
verses.bsb: And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, He explained to them what was written in all the Scriptures about Himself.

verses.kjv: And they drew nigh unto the village, whither they went: and he made as though he would have gone further.
verses.bsb: As they approached the village where they were headed, He seemed to be going farther.

verses.kjv: But they constrained him, saying, Abide with us: for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent. And he went in to tarry with them.
verses.bsb: But they pleaded with Him, “Stay with us, for it is nearly evening and the day is almost over.”  So He went in to stay with them.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread, and blessed it, and brake, and gave to them.
verses.bsb: While He was reclining at the table with them, He took bread, spoke a blessing and broke it, and gave it to them.

verses.kjv: And their eyes were opened, and they knew him; and he vanished out of their sight.
verses.bsb: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized Jesus—and He disappeared from their sight.

verses.kjv: And they said one to another, Did not our heart burn within us, while he talked with us by the way, and while he opened to us the scriptures?
verses.bsb: They asked each other, “Were not our hearts burning within us as He spoke with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?”

verses.kjv: And they rose up the same hour, and returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and them that were with them,
verses.bsb: And they got up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem.  There they found the Eleven and those with them, gathered together

verses.kjv: Saying, The Lord is risen indeed, and hath appeared to Simon.
verses.bsb: and saying, “The Lord has indeed risen and has appeared to Simon!”

verses.kjv: And they told what things were done in the way, and how he was known of them in breaking of bread.
verses.bsb: Then the two told what had happened on the road, and how they had recognized Jesus in the breaking of the bread.

verses.kjv: ¶ And as they thus spake, Jesus himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
verses.bsb: While they were describing these events, Jesus Himself stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”

verses.kjv: But they were terrified and affrighted, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.
verses.bsb: But they were startled and frightened, thinking they had seen a spirit.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Why are ye troubled? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?
verses.bsb: “Why are you troubled,” Jesus asked, “and why do doubts arise in your hearts?

verses.kjv: Behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself: handle me, and see; for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.
verses.bsb: Look at My hands and My feet. It is I Myself. Touch Me and see—for a spirit does not have flesh and bones, as you see I have.”

verses.kjv: And when he had thus spoken, he shewed them his hands and his feet.
verses.bsb: And when He had said this, He showed them His hands and feet.

verses.kjv: And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
verses.bsb: While they were still in disbelief because of their joy and amazement, He asked them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”

verses.kjv: And they gave him a piece of a broiled fish, and of an honeycomb.
verses.bsb: So they gave Him a piece of broiled fish,

verses.kjv: And he took it, and did eat before them.
verses.bsb: and He took it and ate it in front of them.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, These are the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you, that all things must be fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to them, “These are the words I spoke to you while I was still with you: Everything must be fulfilled that is written about Me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms.”

verses.kjv: Then opened he their understanding, that they might understand the scriptures,
verses.bsb: Then He opened their minds to understand the Scriptures.

verses.kjv: And said unto them, Thus it is written, and thus it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise from the dead the third day:
verses.bsb: And He told them, “This is what is written: The Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,

verses.kjv: And that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: and in His name repentance and forgiveness of sins will be proclaimed to all nations, beginning in Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And ye are witnesses of these things.
verses.bsb: You are witnesses of these things.

verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem, until ye be endued with power from on high.
verses.bsb: And behold, I am sending the promise of My Father upon you. But remain in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he led them out as far as to Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.
verses.bsb: When Jesus had led them out as far as Bethany, He lifted up His hands and blessed them.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, while he blessed them, he was parted from them, and carried up into heaven.
verses.bsb: While He was blessing them, He left them and was carried up into heaven.

verses.kjv: And they worshipped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy:
verses.bsb: And they worshiped Him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,

verses.kjv: And were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.
verses.bsb: praising God continually in the temple.
